programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The mother of a model threatens to kill anyone who proposes to her daughter, and becomes the prime suspect when a private investigator is murdered. Lesley-Anne Down guest stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jesse seeks advice from Dr Sloan when he suspects an improper procedure was the cause of a patient's death, and the pair become embroiled in a $2million life insurance fraud, in which a renowned surgeon is the prime suspect

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Richard Kimble is mistaken for a wealthy man and kidnapped by a couple who have vengeance as well as money on their mind

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A man is charged with murder when his girlfriend's stepfather - who disapproved of their relationship - is killed, leaving her free to claim her inheritance

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker enlists Trivette's help and cross the border to save one of his former partners from the Mexican mafia. Adventure, starring Chuck Norris

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Frank steps on a land mine and realises he cannot remove his foot without triggering the device, leaving Horatio and the bomb disposal team facing the delicate task of deactivating it. Meanwhile, Delko breaks up a domestic dispute while off duty - only to become the subject of a lawsuit. Rex Linn and Adam Rodriguez star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An internal security breach forces NCIS to go into lockdown when a covert listening device is found in a pen belonging to Secretary of the Navy Sarah Porter. DiNozzo discovers that National Security Agency analyst Ellie Bishop wrote a report two years previously predicting the possibility of such an occurrence and she is brought in to assist with the investigation and track down the culprit. Guest starring Leslie Hope and Emily Wickersham

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam meet up with former colleague Mike Renko, who is back in town on the trail of a civilian militia he thinks is responsible for the theft of anti-tank missiles from a Navy base. Their discussion is interrupted by Hetty with footage of a man being strangled at an aquarium - and the victim is identified as a lance corporal who was Renko's inside man at the group

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker rushes to the aid of a famous singer being harassed by her ex-husband, and soon finds himself falling for her. Chuck Norris stars

Grace makes a momentous decision about her career and Phil drops a bombshell on his Sun Hill colleagues

programme.desc: Mark feels frustrated at the power vacuum left by Vanessa's resignation as CEO, which has resulted in the porters going on strike. Guest starring Warren Donnelly (Stan in Shameless)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Former Footballers' Wives star Ben Richards joins the cast as PC Nate Roberts, a seasoned professional with an appetite for action and an eye for the ladies. programme.desc: The regiment's tour in Germany is cut short. Meanwhile, a prank in the Officer's Mess causes trouble for Tucker. Starring Robson Green and Rob Spendlove

Starring Richard Griffiths and Samantha Janus

programme.title: Lovejoy

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Lionel struggles to cope without Veronica, and Jacko suspects that Sandra's shifty new boyfriend is taking advantage of their boss's low mood to help himself to the petty cash

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mr Grace decides to move with the times by making an advertisement to be shown in local cinemas. Classic comedy, starring John Inman and the late Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy criticises local folklore and upsets Compo and Clegg by condemning a Yorkshire god called Earnshaw. However, subsequent events suggest he has been foolish to voice his opinions. Long-running comedy, starring Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Part one of two. Compo yearns to reach for the skies in a hang-glider to impress Nora Batty, but finds a challenge to climb a tree more than enough to handle. Meanwhile, the object of his affections pesters Wally to take her out for a meal. Long-running comedy set in rural Yorkshire, starring Bill Owen and Kathy Staff

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The poisoning of a Lancashire vicar appears to be a tragic accident until Lynley and Havers delve deeper into his past and discover a sinister motive for murder. Meanwhile, the detective's friendship with profiler Helen Clyde looks set to take on a new dimension. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The ex-coppers find themselves under the microscope when a psychologist arrives to conduct a study into older men in the workplace - but she soon reveals a more personal reason for her visit. It turns out that five years ago her brother, a cycle courier, was the victim of a hit-and-run that left him unable to recall anything prior to the incident. Now he says he was targeted for the parcel he was carrying, which was stolen at the scene - or so he believes. Sally Phillips guest stars

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The discovery of human remains in an urban canal is linked to the unsolved murder of a prison governor 15 years earlier, leading the team to seek out the original suspects. However, it is not long before the prison psychologist, who is married to the victim's daughter, finds himself personally embroiled in the investigation. Meanwhile, Boyd launches a search for his son Luke

programme.desc: Young Mr Grace opens a social club for his employees, and they decide to decorate it in their own inimitable style. John Inman, Mollie Sugden, and Trevor Bannister star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy, Clegg and Compo try to make Sid appear dashing in Ivy's eyes, convincing Weasley to use him in his latest experiment. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde, John Comer and Gordon Wharmby

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy persuades Clegg and Compo that starting a dog-walking business will be no work at all since they are already going on daily countryside rambles. However, the experience proves to be more of a workout than the trio envisage. Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen star

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An old friend of Dalziel arouses suspicion by claiming a series of apparently accidental deaths may have a more sinister explanation. Inquiries lead the duo into a tangled web of family loyalties - and onto the trail of a mild-mannered rose-grower who could be a serial killer. Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan star, with Keith Barron

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team investigates the mystery of a missing scientist, after the man's wife receives a series of emails from someone claiming to know what happened to him. The physics professor was last seen in 2004, on a train to Paris for a conference, and when he disappeared he was working on a potentially lucrative cold fusion project that had attracted attention from many large organisations. However, as the detectives dig deeper, a shadowy Whitehall figure warns Sandra's boss Strickland they should drop the case

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An anonymous e-mail leads the squad to wasteground in east London, where the remains of a right-wing political activist are found. Further research into the man's past reveals he was not who he claimed to be and had a hidden agenda of his own. Detective drama, guest starring Philip Whitchurch and Cyril Nri

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Schizophrenic Michael Simms threatens Shelley Cooper with a knife and when the woman is later killed, Neil orders CO19 to shoot him, assuming Simms is the culprit. In the subsequent inquiry by the Department of Professional Standards, the DI's judgement is called into question by his own superior officer and he faces the prospect that his career could be over

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The 12th-century sleuth sets out to clear his novice Oswin of murdering a nun whose body has been found frozen in a block of ice. While tracking down the culprit, he is also forced to confront the fact he fathered a son before he took holy orders. Medieval mystery based on the novels of Ellis Peters, starring Derek Jacobi, Mark Charnock, Eoin McCarthy and Julian Firth

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max is determined to get Linda back at any cost - but where does Phil fit into his plan? Jean encourages Sharon to give things another go with Phil, although Kat tells her she could do better. Iqra has shocking news for Martin, and after a heavy night's drinking, Peter turns superhero in his attempt to impress Ash

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom's life is hanging in the balance, while Hanssen questions whether he did the right thing. Ange is devastated when she learns that things have not gone to plan, and Dom will face life-changing consequences of his injuries. Sacha vows to save Jodie's baby, and begins to understand the ongoing impact of her relationship with her mother. Cameron announces his intention to make a career change

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Documentary following Scottish Ambulance Service crews in Glasgow and Edinburgh as they respond to 999 calls. In a remote rural location, a small child falls in a loch, triggering a huge emergency response, while a motorway crash sees several people hospitalised, including a pregnant woman

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts the semi-final of the cooking challenge. Mary Berry challenges the remaining contestants to prepare a fish supper, Chris Bavin asks them to come up with a meal that puts mushrooms front and centre, and Angela Hartnett sets them the task of making crab ravioli with homemade pasta dough. After a dramatic end to the final challenge, one more celebrity leaves the kitchen

programme.title: Music's Dirty Secrets: Women Fight Back
programme.desc: Reporter Tamanna Rahman investigates how safe the music industry is for women, investigating the cases of a grime star currently serving 30 years in prison for rape, and a woman who shared her account of abuse and the hands of a rising rap star, leading to the cancellation of his debut album

programme.title: Lily: A Transgender Story
programme.desc: Filmed over five years, cameras follow 20-year-old Lily Jones, as she transitions from male to female. During this period she leaves her seaside home in Aberystwyth for a new life in Birmingham, undergoes gender reassignment surgery and finds love

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A former bank in Crewe is sold, a flood-damaged home in Cumbria is bought to benefit the community, and in Tooting, south London, a developer has grand plans for a two-bedroom flat

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Cassie Thorn is convinced moving to Australia will be perfect for her family, but her husband Aaron is not so sure, so they and their children sample life in Brisbane for a week

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: PC Claire Bond battles to get back to work after a criminal driver left her with life-changing injuries. Plus, a police officer shot at close range risks losing his sight

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Doctors

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Max and Miranda investigate the brutal murder of the famous British author Nicholas Mountford, and investigate the possible link to a bull fighting ban

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah heads to the north Devon countryside to help a career minded couple with £950,000 to spend on a family home and start up a holiday-let business. While in the county, Sonali visits the UK's first Unesco Biosphere Reserve to learn about the long term ambitions to connect people and nature, and efforts to conserve one of the most fragile grassland areas in the south west

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea's imaginations are put to the test when they make different versions of children's furnuiture. The duo also revamp an old lampshade and make a simple key holder. Plus, from the archives, Alan Titchmarsh returfs a lawn, and Joe Swift and Carol Klein reveal their favourite tools for gardening

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the show in which five dealers bid to buy a unique 1960s vase, a curious mirror, two chalkware statues and a troll called Trotter

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes Yorkshireman Ged, who has brought in a classic piece of industrial furniture saved from the scrap heap. Henrie wants to sell a silver and mahogany Chinese inkwell from the early 19th century. And Molly from Manchester is selling a a watchman's timepiece

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, Peter Mason from Herefordshire arrives with an old-fashioned ice cream maker that belonged to his beloved Granny Ethel, and holds memories of happy times at her house churning vanilla ice cream together. Meanwhile, organ restorer David Burville takes on a Spanish barrel organ, known as an organillo. Later, the experts restore a portable 1950s record player and a leather document case

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts as the remaining three contestants take part in the final as they compete to be crowned the first-ever winner of the competition, with the cooks having to impress Mary Berry, Angela Hartnett and Chris Bavin if they're to win the title. First, the competitors take on the Rustle Up challenge, creating dishes that showcase butternut squash as the main ingredient, before being asked to produce their ultimate picnic and presenting their creations to the judges at a beautifully decorated picnic tables. Who will be crowned the winner?

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: The Only Way is Essex's James Lock and Yazmin Oukhellou talk about their failed relationship, and Ru Paul's Drag Race star Vinegar Strokes reunites with first love, Emile

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Megan Barton-Hanson meets up with her ex Demi Sims, and Olympian Ashley Mckenzie comes face-to-face with his ex, Danielle

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic hockey gold medallist Alex Danson-Bennett, former England fast-bowler Steve Harmison, world champion swimmer Karen Pickering and ex-Everton and Arsenal striker Kevin Campbell join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Mother Sarah thinks it would be easier to raise her three-year-old triplets in Australia - but stay-at-home father Conor has major concerns. programme.title: Critical Incident

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al receives a disconcerting call, while Daniel is unimpressed when Zara spreads an intimate secret. Al gives relationship advice to a painter and her self-medicating boyfriend

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Max and Miranda are determined to catch a wanted man who fled the island after killing his girlfriend, having learned he has returned to pay his respects to his dying father

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor is property hunting in Shropshire with a couple of keen gardeners who want their £425,000 to buy them a country home with ample space for them and their hot tub. While in the county, Margherita samples some of Shropshire's pioneering local produce at Chadwell Park Farm, which has successfully turned its hand to making vegetable juices from its salad crops

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: An old trunk is turned into a cocktail cabinet, Diarmuid Gavin creates a garden retreat complete with firepit, and a 50p charity shop cup makes an extraordinary return at auction

programme.title: The Bidding Room

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With the evidence stacked against him, Phil is running out of options - and things only get worse when Max starts piling on the pressure. Linda is furious after realising that Jack told his brother about Mick's abuse. Martin asks Kush for advice after Iqra's revelation about Ruby, and Jean and Sharon have some fun at Suki's expense

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents as the second group of quarter-finalists give some scruffy bichons frisés their trademark white cloud look, and try to identify a cross-breed's two parents. In the final challenge, they must trim their dogs' coats to create a homage to a place close to their hearts, before showing off their creations on the Dogwalk. But who will judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor take through to next week?

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: A man confesses to murdering his boss, despite being unable to remember what happened. Convinced that things are not as simple as they seem, Neville determines to uncover the truth and unmask the real killer. Detective drama, starring Ralf Little

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Barnstaple in Devon to help Jan and Lucy, whose daily four-hour round trips to hospital for life-saving dialysis treatment for two of their daughters is taking a heavy toll on family life. Along with designer Laurence Llewelyn Bowen and hundreds of local volunteers, Nick and the crew extend their house to create a home dialysis centre, enabling the girls to be treated where they live and letting the whole family settle into a healthier and happier routine

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Raj Bisram help two teams look for items at Bath Racecourse, while presenter Eric Knowles talks about jewellery with expert Izzie Balmer

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades and Dominic Chinea demonstrate DIY tips and tricks that will not break the bank and delve into the BBC archives for advice from the nation's favourite experts. They begin by creating free-standing shelves using scaffolding poles and an old set of drawers

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Padstow is well known for its tourist industry, but there's another lucrative industry here that few visitors are aware of. Each year, Padstow's fishermen land hundreds of tonnes of brown crab. But the vast majority bypass the town altogether. For decades, lorryloads of shellfish have left Padstow for the continent every week. Once connected to Europe, Padstow's crabbing industry boomed. Britain's crab exports to Europe form an industry worth over sixty million pounds. But lockdowns across Europe have brought everything to a standstill, and as the global market for crab grows, concerns are starting to be raised about the effects of overfishing

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright shows us exercises designed to make the body strong

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Iain and Vikki from Loch Lomond take on Antonia and Charlie as they go head to head to determine who will meet today's curious collector Myk. Paul Martin hosts

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Jonty Hearnden and Danny Sebastian battle for lots at a London auction house. While Jonty finds himself in a world of fashion, Danny muscles in on his territory

programme.title: The Great British Winter
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison visits the Lake District to explore how its landscape and wildlife are affected by Britain's changeable weather. During her journey, she encounters a rare Arctic winter resident and witnesses a lakeside spectacle performed by a family of otters

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Katherine Downes presents action from day nine of the first Grand Slam event of the year, featuring the opening quarter-finals at Melbourne Park

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Nikita defends her land when the Whiskers cross into Commando territory

programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes with animals and keepers at Edinburgh Zoo and the Highland Wildlife Park, beginning as a new arrival joins the resident dik-dik family. World Rhino Day gives staff a chance to let their hair down and have some fun as they raise both money and awareness of conservation issues

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The eight remaining interior designers take part in a commercial design challenge to transform a country wedding venue in Sussex. Presented by Alan Carr with Judge Michelle Ogundehin

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: When a 15-year-old teenager is fatally stabbed in a park in Coventry, the forensics team must identify the murderers through evidence left behind at the crime scene. However, the weapon is nowhere to be found, so they are reliant on just two items - a mobile phone found a short distance from the body and the victim's blood-soaked jacket

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, joined virtually by a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life, who are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined by special guests Mo Gilligan and Katherine Ryan, who will be beamed into the studio to chew over the lighter end of the news spectrum

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories. With Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The presenter discusses the challenge of preserving unspoilt wildernesses, from the icy expanses of Siberia to the tropical forests of central and South America. Simon reflects on how his past series have dealt with the causes and impact of climate change, as well as remembering a memorable report exposing the extent of plastic pollution in the ocean. He also recalls meeting indigenous people around the world and hearing their first-hand experiences of ecological damage

programme.title: DeLorean: Back from the Future
programme.desc: Documentary charting the rise and fall of John Z DeLorean and his doomed attempt to build the sports car of the future in 1980s war-torn Belfast. The programme features rare and unseen footage filmed by Oscar winning directors DA Pennebaker and Chris Hegedus, offering the first in-depth psychological profile of DeLorean

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Following on from the innovative series Your Home Made Perfect, Angela Scanlon returns with this show that offers the same hi-tech makeover technology to outdoor spaces. First up are Demi and Laura from Hertfordshire, who lead a busy family life, meaning they haven't had time to deal with their lacklustre, L-shaped plot. Garden designers Helen Elks-Smith and Manoj Malde pitch for the chance to redesign the site - demonstrating what they would do with virtual reality presentations that put the couple right at the heart of their new, improved outdoor havens. But which one will they choose?

programme.desc: Ellie Harrison continues her exploration of Britain's most extreme winter landscapes, searching for beauty behind the bleakness within forest environments. She encounters inhabitants including red squirrels and Eurasian beavers, and discovers that during these unforgiving months vital tasks including charcoal creation and tree planting get under way

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: The giant pandas keep everyone on their toes and monkey Dennis gets hurt in a fight. A tiny insect is at the heart of one conservation project, beavers at the core of another

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the remote Stoer Peninsula in the North West Highlands, Helen and Graham are relieved to see tourists returning to the area, bringing a very welcome financial boost with them. However, they are soon forced to make a decision with devastating consequences. In Northumberland National Park, Emma and Ewan attempt to shear all their sheep, and in the Lake District, Matt and Murray embark on a dramatic gather

programme.title: Trump Takes On the World
programme.desc: After one of the most shocking presidencies in history, Donald Trump's top advisers and the leaders who clashed with him lift the lid on the critical moments of his foreign policy. In the second programme, Trump turns his attention to the Middle East, where he takes America to the brink of war with Iran, shocks the world by pulling troops out of Syria, and announces breakthrough agreements between Israel and Arab states

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Actress and comedian Kerry Godliman, star of ITV's Bad Move and Taskmaster on Dave, comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. She warms up the audience before introducing routines by the energetic Suzi Ruffell and South African comedian Loyiso Gola

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: When a 15-year-old teenager is fatally stabbed in a park in Coventry, the forensics team must identify the murderers through evidence left behind at the crime scene. However, the weapon is nowhere to be found, so they are reliant on just two items - a mobile phone found a short distance from the body and the victim's blood-soaked jacket

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at the effect human activity is having, revealing how the carbon dioxide being produced is destabilising life on Earth. There is footage of rescued orphaned elephants in Kenya, victims of ever-worsening droughts, while ocean patrols off the coast of Gabon fight to save endangered sharks. In the Amazon, wildlife teams are saving animals in the shrinking rainforest, and in San Diego a zoo is freezing the DNA of endangered species before they go extinct

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Alan Carr hosts the interior design challenge, in which 10 aspiring designers compete to redesign an Oxfordshire show home. Michelle Ogundehin assesses their efforts, and is joined in the first episode by guest judge Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen

Plus, in a new challenge, the contestants' knowledge of crossbreeds is put to the test as they try to identify a dog's parents. Presented by Sheridan Smith, with judges Verity Hardcastle and Colin Taylor

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: Jonty Hearnden and Danny Sebastian search for bargains at Ardingly Antiques Fair in West Sussex, where their interests are piqued by a vintage railway sign and an antique telescope

programme.title: The Great British Winter
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison visits Britain's largest estuarine habitat in Morecambe Bay to discover the best-kept secrets of the winter landscape, including an unusual gentlemen's club of grey seals on Walney Island. She also reveals why both birds and fishermen are attracted to the area during the harsh weather

programme.title: Tennis: Australian Open
programme.desc: Katherine Downes presents action from day 11 of the Grand Slam tournament at Melbourne Park in Australia, featuring both women's semi-finals and the opening men's last-four match

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: During her final days of pregnancy, Rocket Dog searches for a new burrow in which to give birth, while Zorro experiences life as a Commando

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Rocket Dog's pups are put in jeopardy when babysitters Sophie and Axel become distracted by Wilson - before an even greater threat emerges

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Barrow Hill Roundhouse near Chesterfield in Derbyshire. Antiques experts Jonathan Pratt and Raj Bisram are on hand to find a selection of objects to take to auction. Jonathan comes across a collection of 1950s powder compacts, while Raj discovers some railway signal lamps. Paul finds out about the history of the Roundhouse and gets hands on with some locomotive maintenance

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Marcus Brigstocke, Anna Richardson, Tom Rosenthal and Sindhu Vee test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary Beard talks to cultural creatives who make other worlds for people's exploration and delight. Booker Prize-winner Marlon James discusses his Dark Star trilogy, which turns the fantasy genre on its head, and David Greig, artistic director of Edinburgh's Lyceum Theatre, takes viewers on a quest to an ancient woodland, where appealing to the spirits seems completely rational during a pandemic. Mary also chairs a debate on whether the occult is enjoying a revival or if it has always been more popular than she imagines

programme.title: Nigella: At My Table
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares ideas for feasts throughout the day, with her savoury take on French toast an ideal choice for weekend brunch or a comforting and quick supper. With friends to entertain for dinner, she rustles up an informal Mediterranean dish of juicy spiced lamb kofta with garlic sauce, followed by rose and pepper pavlova with strawberries. There are also recipes for an aromatic golden egg curry and a white chocolate cheesecake

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Pam and Marcus have a big garden surrounded by large established trees, which stretches away from their suburban home in St Albans, Hertfordshire. Now their children have grown up, they want to transform the garden into a space they they can enjoy themselves. Designer Helen Elks-Smith is set on a design that begins with the home, while rival Tom Massey wants to transport them to another world. Which idea will the couple choose? Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Monica Galetti and Giles Coren visit the Yorkshire Dales to work at Swinton Estate, the ancestral home of Lord and Lady Masham. By taking a look the heart of the grand house and its countryside charm, as well as the wider estate, which has opened up to for guests to enjoy, Monica and Giles learn about the challenges of making history pay in the modern world

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A musical review of 1990, the year that saw hip-hop and dance music hit the charts with a passion thanks to the likes of Adamski, Orbital, 808 State, Snap! and Eurodance sensations Snap! Betty Boo, Beats International and MC Tunes introduced the British take on hip-hop, Sinead O'Connor and Maria McKee took to the stage with big ballads and both John Barnes and Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles showed their rapping skills. Milli Vanilli got caught up in a singing scandal, and the battle for the Christmas number one saw a stand-off between Cliff Richard and Vanilla Ice

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emma Barnett

programme.title: Trump Takes On the World
programme.desc: After one of the most shocking presidencies in history, Donald Trump's top advisers and the leaders who clashed with him lift the lid on the critical moments of his foreign policy. In the second programme, Trump turns his attention to the Middle East, where he takes America to the brink of war with Iran, shocks the world by pulling troops out of Syria, and announces breakthrough agreements between Israel and Arab states

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: The second part of this two-part documentary explores the initial outbreak of Covid-19 in the US. When Covid-19 first arrived on American soil, US federal health authorities were seemingly unaware of how transmissible the virus truly was. This account chronicles 54 days in which the United States made crucial decisions that would severely impact the spread of the virus. Featuring interviews with public health officials and scientists who had already concluded that the virus had global pandemic potential, who were confronted with a failing diagnostic test and a president who appeared to repeatedly undermine the true threat of the virus

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman hosts a star-studded version of the culinary contest, which sees Mary Berry, Chris Bavin and Angela Hartnett judging 10 famous cooks. Taking part are Ferne McCann, Gareth Thomas, Desiree Burch, Ed Balls, Karim Zeroual, Ruth Madeley, Tom Read Wilson, Shobna Gulati, Ed Byrne and Rachel Johnson. In this first edition, Mary wants to know what home cooking means to the contestants, so she challenges them to produce their ultimate home-cooked dinner

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The remaining nine talented celebrity home cooks are put through their paces as they face a series of challenges. First, Mary Berry tasks the famous faces to make a chocolate dessert fit for a romantic date night, before the competitors take on Chris Bavin's tricky challenge of rustling up a dish using his favourite cut of leftover meat. The judges must then decide, taking both challenges into account, who is safe and who needs to prove themselves by cooking again in Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe shows what can be learned from the ways in which animals adapt to changes in the world around them

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a countryside scene, complete with a big red barn and a rickety wooden fence

programme.title: The Golden Age of Canals
programme.desc: Documentary charting the changing face of Britain's canals after the Second World War, as enthusiasts fought to save the inland waterways from destruction - despite facing strong opposition from the government. Amateur and professional footage illustrates the canals' transformation from industrial thoroughfares to tourist attractions during the 1950s and 1960s, as well as efforts to restore and preserve the network's locks and boats. Narrated by Gina McKee

programme.title: Britain's Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.desc: Andy Torbet and local seafaring volunteers build a boat made of just willow and cow hide, and set out to cross the dangerous Pentland Firth as the ancient Orcadians would have done. Neil Oliver investigates the extraordinary discovery of some human bones, Chris Packham goes in search of whales and Shini Somara uncovers the powers of the tides

programme.title: Retreat: Meditations from a Monastery
programme.desc: The second episode follows the a typical day in the life of the 21 monks residing in Pluscarden Abbey, a remote monastery on the edge of the Scottish Highlands in Moray. Given its isolated location, this Abbey is almost entirely self-sufficient, and the monks make their own clothes and grow their own crops, giving them little reason to have contact with the outside world

programme.title: The Last Igloo
programme.desc: Documentary following a day in the life of an Inuit hunter in Greenland, who builds one of the traditional ice dwellings while fishing through the sea ice. While igloos are no longer used as permanent homes, the traditional skills behind their construction are still used in building temporary shelters in the wilderness. The film captures this ingenious craft, which may be lost to the world as the ice sheet disappears

programme.title: Britain and the Sea
programme.desc: David Dimbleby continues his voyage around Britain, sailing his yacht Rocket along coastal regions of the South East, the first point of attack for enemy forces and therefore essential to the nation's defence throughout history. Along the way, he takes a tour of HMS Victory, Nelson's flagship vessel from the Battle of Trafalgar, lovingly preserved in dry dock in Portsmouth. He also meets a carpenter who still makes wooden figureheads, discovers how a chamber pot acted as propaganda in the battle against Napoleon and reveals the way abstract art was used to deceive German submarine pilots

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a countryside scene, complete with a big red barn and a rickety wooden fence

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe shows what can be learned from the ways in which animals adapt to changes in the world around them

programme.title: The Golden Age of Canals
programme.desc: Documentary charting the changing face of Britain's canals after the Second World War, as enthusiasts fought to save the inland waterways from destruction - despite facing strong opposition from the government. Amateur and professional footage illustrates the canals' transformation from industrial thoroughfares to tourist attractions during the 1950s and 1960s, as well as efforts to restore and preserve the network's locks and boats. Narrated by Gina McKee

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: Immersive viewing experience, featuring a combination of sequences from natural history programmes with narration from Buddhist monk Andy Puddicombe

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates another masterpiece of powerful clouds, rolling hills and winding river waters using only his special painting knife

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Vanessa Collingridge examines the life of Elizabeth Tudor, with a focus on how documentary TV and the BBC have looked at her legacy and interrogated her reign. Using archive stretching back over 60 years, her upbringing, her conflicts with enemies including Mary Queen of Scots, and her greatest victory against the Spanish Armada are investigated. The programme seeks to understand how the monarch created a legacy that people still live with today, and how it has changed throughout the centuries

programme.title: Elizabeth R
programme.desc: Award-winning drama series chronicling the life of Queen Elizabeth I, beginning with the 10-year period preceding her accession to the throne at the age of 25. Starring Glenda Jackson, Ronald Hines, Daphne Slater, Rachel Kempson, Bernard Hepton and Peter Jeffrey

programme.title: Retreat: Meditations from a Monastery
programme.desc: Life in Belmont Abbey, a Benedictine monastery in Herefordshire, where monks including a renowned iconographer and another who creates manuscripts and rosaries

programme.title: All Aboard! New Zealand by Rail, Sea and Land
programme.desc: Cameras take an epic overland journey, by train, boat and car through New Zealand's breathtaking landscapes. The trek beings in Auckland, but the city soon gives way to rolling pastures, volcanic extremes, tranquil waterways, the snowcapped grandeur of the Southern Alps and the beauty of Fiordland

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates another masterpiece of powerful clouds, rolling hills and winding river waters using only his special painting knife

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nicholas Crane explores the history of England's largest island, the Isle of Wight, learning how the fossil-rich area was once attached to the mainland and contains clues to the geographical journey made by the whole of Britain

programme.title: Mindful Escapes: Breathe, Release, Restore
programme.desc: The natural world at rest offers a constant source of calm and comfort, helping people to experience more being and less doing. Mindfulness expert Andy Puddicombe talks viewers through the process and onto an immersive journey around the sights and sounds of resting wildlife all over the planet

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a landscape with a glowing evening sky on top of a black canvas

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: James manages to settle into the routine of his new life - but then meets Helen, the beautiful daughter of a local farmer. Siegfried decides to bring some business-like discipline to the practice by employing the formidable Mrs Harbottle as secretary

programme.title: Edie (2017)
programme.desc: Seeking to fulfil a decades-old dream to climb a mountain in the Scottish Highlands, Edith Moore, an 83-year-old widow, escapes to Scotland, where she grudgingly accepts the help of Jonny, the manager of a struggling shop that sells outdoor gear. Drama, starring Sheila Hancock and Kevin Guthrie

programme.title: Sheila Hancock Brushes Up: The Art of Watercolours
programme.desc: The actress presents a history of watercolour painting, discovering a catalogue of little-known works and exploring how the medium's immediacy and freedom has given it an enduring appeal for both amateur and professional artists. She visits locations including the Alps, India and Venice to tell the stories behind paintings that were created there, as well as examining the importance of watercolours to artists in the days before photography

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Treasures
programme.desc: Dan Cruickshank examines man-made wonders in Mexico and the USA, beginning with the Mayan burial pyramids of Palenque, where he uncovers stories of human sacrifice. Travelling north, he visits Mesa Verde, a settlement dug out of the cliffs by an unknown ancient civilisation, and the most famous American symbol - the Statue of Liberty. Also featured are the Toltec statues at Tula, Thomas Jefferson's house and the 1851 Navy Colt revolver

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary about a husband-and-wife team who revolutionised broadcast news in America by capturing breaking news stories in Los Angeles from their helicopter. Their cameras captured not only the adrenaline of live news culture, but also the strain it began to take on their relationship, which ultimately led to a major life transition for one of them

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Mr Hammond is on a wildlife trip in New Zealand, and Andy is eager to hear what animals he has spotted. Jen and Andy call Mr Hammond from the Aqua Base, who answers their call standing in a river, wearing full fishing gear. He informs Jen and Andy that he has seen killer whales and is fishing for trout. Suddenly, there is a barking noise and Mr Hammond looks in disbelief as he sees a fur seal pup disappear into the forest. Andy reassures a distressed Mr Hammond that he and Jen will investigate why the seal pup is making its way into the forest. It’s time to go on an aquatic adventure! programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Jen has just got back from a bird-spotting trip in the Galapagos Islands, and Andy is keen to learn how it went. Jen tells Andy that thanks to the pocket bird book Mr Hammond lent her she was able to spot lots of sea birds she had never seen before. Andy is interested in the book and asks to see it, but unfortunately on the last day of Jen’s trip a waved albatross swopped down and carried it away. Mr Hammond calls from his new office in the Galapagos dome and is wearing ear protectors due to a peculiar loud noise which he’d like them to investigate. He also asks Jen for his bird book so that he can do some bird spotting from his new office. Andy informs Mr Hammond that Jen is still unpacking so will bring the bird book over shortly. programme.title: BBC World News
programme.desc: The latest international news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Exiles: Hong Kong at a Crossroads
programme.desc: In June 2020, after months of increasingly violent protests in Hong Kong, Beijing imposed a sweeping national security law. Critics say it effectively curtails protest and freedom of speech - China has said it will return stability. In response, the UK has introduced a new visa that will give 70% of its former colony's population - 5.4m people - the right to live in the UK, and eventually become citizens. Many activists now face an agonising choice - to stay or to go.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: With schools, colleges and universities around the world closed by Covid-19, the pandemic is blowing a hole in the lives of young people, their education and their life prospects. Remote learning can accentuate the differences between the haves and the have-nots. Time out of school can also mean a lack of decent food for the poorest and increase the risk of domestic violence against girls. Higher education has been thrown into disarray, with those on the cusp of graduation entering an uncertain job market. How can we stop this cruel disruption to young lives becoming a global generational disaster?

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Marianna Spring looks at the scare tactics of anti-vaxxers, revealing the scale of a social media blitz that has targeted vulnerable people and is now reaching younger generations yet to be called for their vaccination. The programme witnesses the reaction of a test group exposed to one anti-vax video, all under the watchful eye of one of the UK's most respected doctors. Will they be influenced by disinformation or will their plans to be vaccinated remain unchanged?

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Human rights activist Azimjan Askarov was imprisoned in Kyrgyzstan in 2010 for a crime he says he did not commit. Ever since then, his wife Hadicha has campaigned tirelessly for his release. Now she sees one last chance for justice, in an appeal hearing at the country's supreme court. But as Hadicha prepares for the verdict, coronavirus is spreading across Kyrgyzstan, stopping Hadicha's prison visits and putting 69-year-old Azimjan at risk. Our World follows an extraordinary story of love, courage - and never giving up.

programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a family team of five answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Farooq, Neil, Carys and Emily, who have never met before, pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy is determined, Manpreet tries to reach out to Rishi, and Charles tries to convince Rishi

programme.title: Australian Wilderness with Ray Mears
programme.desc: Ray heads deep into the Australian Outback discover how both people and animals have developed ways to thrive in otherwise challenging conditions. He begins in Western Australia, where 20 million years ago, the sea bed erupted to create the great limestone cliffs of Ningaloo. Amid the rocks, the presenter encounters the fossils of giant prehistoric sharks, before swimming with their modern descendants - the whale sharks - out on Ningaloo reef

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Home Sweet Home - Travels in My Own Land
programme.desc: After a lifetime of travels that have taken her across the globe, Joanna Lumley is making her most personal journey yet. The final episode takes Joanna from North Wales to St Michael's Mount and the West Country, ending in her home city of London

programme.title: Marcella
programme.desc: The detective investigates the truth about Bobby. Trying to track down her own stalker leads to a shocking truth. Stacey attempts to escape the family with her baby. Crime drama, starring Anna Friel

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Piers welcomes Gemma Collins for an emotional, surprising and hilarious interview. The queen of reality recounts how she was bullied as a teenager, suffered a miscarriage and has experienced mental health problems. She also talks about living with fellow The Only Way is Essex star Arg and how she spends the money she has made

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: Following an unexpected turn in the case, Lisa and the team struggle to keep their heads in the game as they are forced to accept help from a new unit. As one suspect throws new light on the investigation, Lisa doubts whether she has been given the full truth on events from the past. With the investigative pressure building, Manning attempts to fight for his marriage, and Lisa finds solace in a place she least expected

programme.title: Australian Wilderness with Ray Mears
programme.desc: Ray heads deep into the Australian Outback discover how both people and animals have developed ways to thrive in otherwise challenging conditions. He begins in Western Australia, where 20 million years ago, the sea bed erupted to create the great limestone cliffs of Ningaloo. Amid the rocks, the presenter encounters the fossils of giant prehistoric sharks, before swimming with their modern descendants - the whale sharks - out on Ningaloo reef

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Home Sweet Home - Travels in My Own Land
programme.desc: After a lifetime of travels that have taken her across the globe, Joanna Lumley is making her most personal journey yet. The final episode takes Joanna from North Wales to St Michael's Mount and the West Country, ending in her home city of London

programme.desc: The detective investigates the truth about Bobby. Trying to track down her own stalker leads to a shocking truth. Stacey attempts to escape the family with her baby. Crime drama, starring Anna Friel

She also talks about living with fellow The Only Way is Essex star Arg and how she spends the money she has made

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: Following an unexpected turn in the case, Lisa and the team struggle to keep their heads in the game as they are forced to accept help from a new unit. programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Meg goes on a Valentine's Day date with a guy she met online. Lois and Peter spend the day lazing in bed, Brian is visited by all of his former girlfriends and Quagmire is transformed into a beautiful woman

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan is upset when Francine has a sexual dream about Jeff and tries to stop her from sleeping

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Fr Donovan organises a Christmas play at the shopping centre, and casts Stan as Santa Claus and Roger as Jesus - but they land themselves in trouble after getting into a fight with each other. Elsewhere, Hayley and Jeff adopt a child, who turns out to be possessed

programme.title: The Stand Up Sketch Show
programme.desc: Routines by comedians including Huge Davies, Judi Love, Alfie Brown, Bobby Mair, Suzi Ruffell, Iain Sterling and Russell Kane are reconstructed as sketches

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy and Dina are both in labour, but while Glenn's manager status gets Dina the royal treatment at the fancy hospital, Amy and Jonah have a far difference experience at a much cheaper clinic. At Cloud 9, Garrett struggles to express his best wishes for the new parents

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Amy is shocked to discover she has no maternity leave and has to return to work two days after her baby is born, while Johah and Garrett interview applicants

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Iain Stirling hosts this game show in which five friends take on Georgia Kousoulou, Ellie Taylor, Jamie Laing, Ben Hanlin and Desiree Burch in a series of challenges

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: A selection of some of the most incredible and peculiar record-breaking attempts, including the longest ears on a rabbit, the most pegs on a person's face and the most beer bottles opened with feet. Matt Edmondson narrates

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fred decides to get on with his life and forget Maureen. Mavis bids an emotional farewell as she leaves for Cartmel. Deirdre goes to the airport to confront Jon

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jon tries his best to explain to Deirdre why he lied. Emily receives a shock when she finds something inappropriate in Ken's house. Maureen instructs Maud to sell the shop

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Paddy vows to do all he can to get Mandy back. Mrs Kirk, however, is intent on sabotaging their relationship. Betty and Jack go to visit Kathy in hospital

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty is convinced that Kathy is on the mend. Mrs Kirk offers Mandy money to stay away from Paddy. Steve is worried whether Kathy will remember the accident

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Village newcomer Vivienne Mackay is found battered to death in her own kitchen after announcing her intention to marry businessman Dominic Bellingham - who has not yet separated from his current wife. PC Mason is reminded of his own mother's death, and personal feelings threaten to cloud his professional judgement. Dawn seizes an opportunity to make a fast buck using Vince's hot dog van on the day of a big football match but finds herself in cut-throat competition with Peggy and her baked potato operation. Nikki Sanderson and Gwen Taylor star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid suffers temporary amnesia, but Jean is on hand to produce reminders of his antics at the annual Boy Scouts reunion. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin gets the chance to play for his college football team - but a cold threatens to earn him an early bath. Flat-share comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: The rent money disappears, forcing Jo to consider selling a prized possession. Flat-share comedy, with Sally Thomsett, Paula Wilcox and Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe and George call a meeting of local farmers to bring to an end their harassment of a neighbour whose unacceptable farming practices have made him deeply unpopular. Dawn falls for good-looking newcomer Billy Garrett, but discovers his charming manner conceals a tortured past that produces sudden angry outbursts. David is hired to help Peggy compile an inventory of house contents of a recently deceased woman, but fears his companion's dodgy dealings have upset the restless spirit of the former resident. Guest starring Phyllis Logan and Tim Brooke-Taylor

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles are not keen on moving to a council estate in Leeds. Steve cannot sleep as he worries about what Kathy remembers. Mandy tells the Dingles about Mrs Kirk's offer

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak compiles a list of eligible bachelors for Mandy to marry. Kim is angry at Steve for attacking Chris. Terry thinks it is his lucky night with Tricia

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre apologises to Ken for doubting him. Leanne and Toyah are amused to hear their house may be haunted. Rita struggles to cope on her own at the Kabin

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jon promises Deirdre he will not blow his second chance with her. Ashley, Gail and Martin attend Don's funeral. Judy starts work at the Kabin, with Katie in the back room

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The naked body of a business executive is found dumped in a rival factory's steriliser machine, leaving Barnaby and Scott with a complicated mystery to unravel. Their investigation begins with factory owners the Plummers, who seem to be hiding more than their share of secrets. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, John Hopkins, Annette Crosbie and Jasper Britton

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A mysterious delivery causes Poirot to recall the time his supper at a Buenos Aires restaurant was rudely interrupted by the murder of a fellow diner, who unwittingly drank from a glass laced with cyanide. Drama, starring David Suchet and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth sets out to solve the baffling murder of a Cambridge don who met with foul play shortly after deciding to change his will. Starring David Suchet, Beth Goddard and Mark Kingston

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A land dispute escalates during a cowboy fair run by the Wild West Society in the village of Ford Florey. Farmers Jack Fincher and Silas Burbage both claim to own a patch of wasteland known as the Swamp, but Dan Malko wants to buy it and create a travellers' site. Barnaby and Jones are called in when the feud leads to murder during the Wild West-style shoot-outs at the annual event. Guest starring Kenneth Cranham, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The son of a high-profile Jewish businessman is attacked in the grounds of a university, leaving Foyle to consider the possibility that the attack was racially motivated. Meanwhile, in London, tensions mount as charismatic right-wing leader Guy Spencer is released from prison. But is he being set up to take the spotlight off a dangerous espionage operation involving MI6? Elsewhere, Sam becomes acutely aware of the impact of the war on Adam's constituents as she encounters a young boy suffering from whooping cough. Michael Kitchen stars in the 1940s-set crime drama, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Daniel Weyman

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack's house burns down, forcing him to move in with a colleague. At work, he becomes involved in the search for a kidnapper after a young woman disappears. Long-running crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Marc Warren

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid's new neighbours are offended by Mike's latest sculpture. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: Jon promises Deirdre he will not blow his second chance with her. Ashley, Gail and Martin attend Don's funeral. Judy starts work at the Kabin, with Katie in the back room

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles are not keen on moving to a council estate in Leeds. Steve cannot sleep as he worries about what Kathy remembers. Mandy tells the Dingles about Mrs Kirk's offer

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak compiles a list of eligible bachelors for Mandy to marry. Kim is angry at Steve for attacking Chris. Terry thinks it is his lucky night with Tricia

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe and George call a meeting of local farmers to bring to an end their harassment of a neighbour whose unacceptable farming practices have made him deeply unpopular. Dawn falls for good-looking newcomer Billy Garrett, but discovers his charming manner conceals a tortured past that produces sudden angry outbursts. David is hired to help Peggy compile an inventory of house contents of a recently deceased woman, but fears his companion's dodgy dealings have upset the restless spirit of the former resident. Guest starring Phyllis Logan and Tim Brooke-Taylor

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Mike attempts to earn some cash without Sid's interference. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin spend an evening at home alone - and the absence of a chaperone allows the trainee chef's amorous side to surface. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin comes home with a puppy, to Chrissy's dismay. Flat-share comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan, Sally Thomsett and Paula Wilcox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague. Carol is not impressed when Rachel is spotted in a clinch with Joe, but he claims it was in the line of duty and completely innocent. Peggy offers to clean for Edward Walton (played by former Likely Lads actor Rodney Bewes), raising Oscar's suspicion she is after his money, but it is Edward's wife, now in a nursing home, who turns out to have a shady past

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy and Butch announce their plan to get married. Steve heads to the hospital to silence Kathy. A fire breaks out at the Woolpack

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail is impressed when Nick tells her he wants to train to be a PE teacher. Les breaks into Emily's and turns the water off. Chris is surprised when Angie wants to finish with him

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rita struggles alone at work when Judy calls in sick. Percy shocks Emily by announcing that he is moving out. Gail is horrified when Nick takes Leanne out

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a priceless music score leads to startling revelations about its composer's life. As Barnaby and Scott investigate, they discover the manuscript may also provide them with the clue they need to solve the case of a local man found battered to death in his cottage. Guest starring June Whitfield and Trevor Peacock

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When a respectable GP is found dead in a public lavatory, the young Morse's outlandish theories see him relegated to general duties by the newly arrived Chief Superintendent Bright. Humiliated and humbled, Endeavour has to investigate from the sidelines, but it's not long before he's questioning DI Fred Thursday's orders and risking his career to avert a family tragedy. Shaun Evans stars, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The last-ever episode. When his colleague Hilda Pierce is targeted by an assassin outside MI5, Foyle must re-examine her top-secret role during the war. He suspects the shooting could be connected to the hunt for a traitor within the Special Operations Executive who may have been behind the deaths of nine agents in France. Meanwhile, Sam is faced with a dilemma when Adam and Glenvil crack down on the black market in East Peckham. Guest stars include Conleth Hill (Game of Thrones' Lord Varys)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is taken hostage while interviewing a suspect, but somehow manages to talk his way out of trouble - quite an achievement for a man whose lack of tact is legendary. Having experienced what it's like to look down a gun barrel, he barges into a nearby Army camp where investigators are trying to solve a soldier's murder, but finds his help isn't appreciated. With David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Sam Kelly, Nicholas Jones and Saira Todd

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is determined to listen to classical music, but someone keeps interrupting. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.desc: Gail is impressed when Nick tells her he wants to train to be a PE teacher. Les breaks into Emily's and turns the water off. Chris is surprised when Angie wants to finish with him

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rita struggles alone at work when Judy calls in sick. Percy shocks Emily by announcing that he is moving out. Gail is horrified when Nick takes Leanne out

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy and Butch announce their plan to get married. Steve heads to the hospital to silence Kathy. A fire breaks out at the Woolpack

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague. programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The family return from holiday, and it seems as if they never went away. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: A housing shortage forces a compromise between the flatmates and their disapproving landlord. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo try to foil a showdown at the Mucky Duck Saloon when Robin and loud Irishman Mick get into an argument. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Eileen Needham sues for divorce after a domestic dispute gets out of hand, and her hard-nosed solicitor Sylvia Swinton (played by Outnumbered actress Samantha Bond) does little to calm the situation, so Joe steps in to play the peacemaker. Stan Bickle arrives in Aidensfield, bringing his daughter Josie (guest star Jenna Boyd), whose flirtatious ways steal David's heart and bring out the worst in Peggy

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The full extent of the damage to the Woolpack is revealed. Kim gives Steve 10,000 pounds to run away with. Kathy tells the police that she saw Steve drive into her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve is questioned about the theft. Kelly is shocked at Mandy's news about her marriage to Butch. Ned gives Robert boxing lessons to help him stand up to bullies

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick signs up to study PE at the college. Emily is upset at Percy's departure. Jon lies to Deirdre and goes back home to his wife Linda for the night

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail hopes Nick will stop seeing Leanne. Angie sobers up, and is horrified that she has moved in with Curly. Sally gets herself dressed up, hoping to spend the evening with Chris

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A house-hunting couple are found dead shortly after viewing a supposedly haunted cottage. Locals claim the property is cursed - but Barnaby and Jones soon hear enough to suspect several builders, developers and estate agents of resorting to murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with George Baker, Ruth Gemmell and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. The next morning she is found dead, the victim of what appears to be a straightforward suicide. However, Lewis is not convinced and digs deeper, identifying several suspects including the site's founder - but as he and Hathaway get closer to the truth amid a rising death toll, they find their personal and professional lives dredged up online for all to see. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Former nanny Lavinia Seagram is found soaked in blood and it is believed she had turned to drink and drugs to commit suicide. However, Poirot is soon on the scent of troubled heiress Norma Restarick, who had problems of her own - not least confessing to the possibility she has killed someone herself. Guest starring Jemima Rooper, Zoe Wanamaker and Peter Bowles, with David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A local gigolo is murdered, forcing Jack to face the uncomfortable fact that some women are willing to pay for sex - and triggering an investigation that takes him into the seedy world of male escorts and their clientele. David Jason stars as the old-fashioned copper, with Isla Blair, Russell Hunter, Joanna David and Martin Jarvis

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid wants a lie-in, but it is doomed from the beginning as Sally chases romance and Jean goes on a diet. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail is impressed when Nick tells her he wants to train to be a PE teacher. Les breaks into Emily's and turns the water off. Chris is surprised when Angie wants to finish with him

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Rita struggles alone at work when Judy calls in sick. Percy shocks Emily by announcing that he is moving out. Gail is horrified when Nick takes Leanne out

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The discovery of a priceless music score leads to startling revelations about its composer's life. As Barnaby and Scott investigate, they discover the manuscript may also provide them with the clue they need to solve the case of a local man found battered to death in his cottage. Guest starring June Whitfield and Trevor Peacock

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Captain Hastings and the Belgian supersleuth investigate a baffling gangland murder which draws them both into the underworld of London's Little Italy. Miss Lemon turns out to have a link with the victim's valet. Whodunit, starring David Suchet and Hugh Fraser

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper sleuth returns to his home country of Belgium for the first time since the First World War to attend a medal ceremony, only to find a murder case needs solving. Agatha Christie mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Chancellor and David De Keyser

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When a respectable GP is found dead in a public lavatory, the young Morse's outlandish theories see him relegated to general duties by the newly arrived Chief Superintendent Bright. Humiliated and humbled, Endeavour has to investigate from the sidelines, but it's not long before he's questioning DI Fred Thursday's orders and risking his career to avert a family tragedy. Shaun Evans stars, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: The last-ever episode. When his colleague Hilda Pierce is targeted by an assassin outside MI5, Foyle must re-examine her top-secret role during the war. He suspects the shooting could be connected to the hunt for a traitor within the Special Operations Executive who may have been behind the deaths of nine agents in France. Meanwhile, Sam is faced with a dilemma when Adam and Glenvil crack down on the black market in East Peckham. Guest stars include Conleth Hill (Game of Thrones' Lord Varys)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is taken hostage while interviewing a suspect, but somehow manages to talk his way out of trouble - quite an achievement for a man whose lack of tact is legendary. Having experienced what it's like to look down a gun barrel, he barges into a nearby Army camp where investigators are trying to solve a soldier's murder, but finds his help isn't appreciated. With David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Sam Kelly, Nicholas Jones and Saira Todd

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is determined to listen to classical music, but someone keeps interrupting. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague.
