programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The family return from holiday, and it seems as if they never went away. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: A housing shortage forces a compromise between the flatmates and their disapproving landlord. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Jo try to foil a showdown at the Mucky Duck Saloon when Robin and loud Irishman Mick get into an argument. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: When a business magnate, his wife and their maid are murdered, Miss Marple (Julia McKenzie) is prompted by the circumstances surrounding the incidents to recall the nursery rhyme Sing a Song of Sixpence. She discovers the victims were part of a family tainted by secrets, and as she ponders the significance of the tune, a past scandal reveals itself. Murder mystery, featuring Wendy Richard as feisty cook Mrs Crump, in her final dramatic role. Other guest stars include Prunella Scales, Matthew Macfadyen, Ralf Little, Rupert Graves and Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Eileen Needham sues for divorce after a domestic dispute gets out of hand, and her hard-nosed solicitor Sylvia Swinton (played by Outnumbered actress Samantha Bond) does little to calm the situation, so Joe steps in to play the peacemaker. Stan Bickle arrives in Aidensfield, bringing his daughter Josie (guest star Jenna Boyd), whose flirtatious ways steal David's heart and bring out the worst in Peggy

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The full extent of the damage to the Woolpack is revealed. Kim gives Steve 10,000 pounds to run away with. Kathy tells the police that she saw Steve drive into her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve is questioned about the theft. Kelly is shocked at Mandy's news about her marriage to Butch. Ned gives Robert boxing lessons to help him stand up to bullies

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Nick signs up to study PE at the college. Emily is upset at Percy's departure. Jon lies to Deirdre and goes back home to his wife Linda for the night

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gail hopes Nick will stop seeing Leanne. Angie sobers up, and is horrified that she has moved in with Curly. Sally gets herself dressed up, hoping to spend the evening with Chris

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A house-hunting couple are found dead shortly after viewing a supposedly haunted cottage. Locals claim the property is cursed - but Barnaby and Jones soon hear enough to suspect several builders, developers and estate agents of resorting to murder. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with George Baker, Ruth Gemmell and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. The next morning she is found dead, the victim of what appears to be a straightforward suicide. However, Lewis is not convinced and digs deeper, identifying several suspects including the site's founder - but as he and Hathaway get closer to the truth amid a rising death toll, they find their personal and professional lives dredged up online for all to see. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Former nanny Lavinia Seagram is found soaked in blood and it is believed she had turned to drink and drugs to commit suicide. However, Poirot is soon on the scent of troubled heiress Norma Restarick, who had problems of her own - not least confessing to the possibility she has killed someone herself. Guest starring Jemima Rooper, Zoe Wanamaker and Peter Bowles, with David Suchet as the Belgian sleuth

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A local gigolo is murdered, forcing Jack to face the uncomfortable fact that some women are willing to pay for sex - and triggering an investigation that takes him into the seedy world of male escorts and their clientele. David Jason stars as the old-fashioned copper, with Isla Blair, Russell Hunter, Joanna David and Martin Jarvis

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid wants a lie-in, but it is doomed from the beginning as Sally chases romance and Jean goes on a diet. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: A girl is followed home from a launderette and abducted. The man responsible seems to want Makepeace find him, which concerns Dempsey.

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A new recruit cannot cope with the stresses of the job and puts the lives of other squad members in jeopardy. A young Lesley Dunlop (Emmerdale's Brenda Walker) features alongside John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed finds himself having to trust agent Melville, who has lost three partners and seems to be lying constantly, as he attempts to convict Lord Edgefield of blackmail. Patrick Macnee stars

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Boxer Lewis Nelson hires Ray to keep his gambling-addicted wife away from the casino he partly owns. However, she persuades Arthur to play the roulette wheels on her behalf, tempting him with the 'winning system' she has devised. Comedy starring George Cole

programme.desc: Henry Cole rides through the south of France, where he finds some of the twistiest roads imaginable. Back home, he meets the designers behind Aston Martin's first ever bike and tells the story of the Suzuki 750, the bike nicknamed 'the Kettle'

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: The prime minister asks Holmes to investigate the disappearance of a letter which could lead to a major European catastrophe. Unfortunately, completion of the task requires him to jeopardise a titled lady's reputation - something he is naturally reluctant to do. Victorian detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: A major South American drugs gang is set to break into the UK trade, but Dempsey's concentration is weakened by the reappearance of an ex-girlfriend from the States. Detective drama, starring Michael Brandon

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A series of damning reports claims police officers are not playing by the book, so Regan poses as an ex-con and heads to a club frequented by two officers suspected of running a protection racket and taking bribes from villains to release them. John Thaw stars

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed is placed under house arrest after being suspected of leaking secrets from the Carmadoc research institute, meaning Tara and her hapless new partner must unearth the real traitor alone

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur acquires an old police coach and launches a sight-seeing business. However, it is not long before the vehicle breaks down and his passengers are forced to seek refuge at the Winchester. Drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole takes a ride through the Cotswolds, savouring its picturesque roads. He also meets the young designers reviving Royal Enfield, tells the story of the Ducati Monster, and has his riding technique assessed

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which a team of five wheelchair basketball enthusiasts answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: The Jager family boat hits the water after being grounded for four months. Skipper Glenn needs money, and he's taking his son with him, but a predator puts their haul in doubt. Plus, Squizzy is on a four-day run to restock his mobile lobster van, but rough seas and the market crisis leave him emotional

programme.title: Blue Streak (1999)
programme.desc: A convicted jewel thief is released from prison, and sets about recovering his hidden loot - only to discover a police station has since been built where he left it. Undeterred, he poses as a maverick cop and worms his way into the detective division - little realising his problems have only just begun. Comedy, starring Martin Lawrence, Luke Wilson and Peter Greene

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The duo find themselves the target of a powerful diamond merchant, forcing Dempsey to enlist the aid of a former boxer he put behind bars

Karl Howman guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed acquires an antique dagger that is said to be a clue to the whereabouts of a priceless treasure. However, everyone who has previously owned the knife has died. Stratford John and Ronald Lacey guest star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: When Ray's van breaks down, Arthur takes to the water using a barge to transport a cargo of dodgy video recorders. However, the fearsome Bible-quoting Welshman who owns them is on his tail. George Cole and Gary Webster star

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole rides an African Twin through the backroads of Oxfordshire, road-tests his first ever electric motorbike, and reveals Alan Milyard's extraordinary new Kawasaki

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Warwick Davis hosts the quiz, with five pub quizzers from south-east London answering questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade 'monster' cases usually involve freshwater culprits, after hearing multiple reports of a deep-water serial killer in Mexico's Sea of Cortez, he decides to step out of his comfort zone and into unchartered depths. The extreme angler meets an attack survivor whose chilling account identifies the underwater assassin as 'el diablo rojo' - the red devil, prompting Jeremy to brave sea-sickening conditions to track down a horrific beast, apparently even more fearsome than sharks

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: Tensions hit boiling point on the William Norling as deckhand Macca makes a monumental blunder that threatens to sink the career of Skipper Danny. Meanwhile, Tasmanian skipper Bryce goes head-to-head with a ferocious storm alone as his deckhand Lachy is sick in bed with a stomach bug.

programme.desc: The seaside resort of Amity is terrorised once again by a man-eating great white shark. Thriller sequel, starring Roy Scheider, Lorraine Gary and Murray Hamilton

programme.desc: Steed recuperates in a private hospital after breaking his leg, unaware an old enemy whom he sent to prison is plotting to murder him. Patrick Macnee and Linda Thorson star, with Ray Brooks

programme.title: Blue Streak (1999)
programme.desc: A convicted jewel thief is released from prison, and sets about recovering his hidden loot - only to discover a police station has since been built where he left it. Comedy, starring Martin Lawrence, Luke Wilson and Peter Greene

programme.title: Demolition Man (1993)

Sci-fi thriller, with Sylvester Stallone, Wesley Snipes, Sandra Bullock and Nigel Hawthorne

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: The duo find themselves the target of a powerful diamond merchant, forcing Dempsey to enlist the aid of a former boxer he put behind bars

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A youth is arrested after an elderly money-lender is brutally assaulted and robbed. His mother asks Regan, an old friend, to intervene. Though it is later proved he got a legitimate loan and is innocent of theft, the youngster finds himself being blackmailed after falling foul of some very dangerous individuals. Karl Howman guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed acquires an antique dagger that is said to be a clue to the whereabouts of a priceless treasure. However, everyone who has previously owned the knife has died. Stratford John and Ronald Lacey guest star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: When Ray's van breaks down, Arthur takes to the water using a barge to transport a cargo of dodgy video recorders. However, the fearsome Bible-quoting Welshman who owns them is on his tail. George Cole and Gary Webster star

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole rides an African Twin through the backroads of Oxfordshire, road-tests his first ever electric motorbike, and reveals Alan Milyard's extraordinary new Kawasaki

programme.title: Tenable

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade 'monster' cases usually involve freshwater culprits, after hearing multiple reports of a deep-water serial killer in Mexico's Sea of Cortez, he decides to step out of his comfort zone and into unchartered depths. The extreme angler meets an attack survivor whose chilling account identifies the underwater assassin as 'el diablo rojo' - the red devil, prompting Jeremy to brave sea-sickening conditions to track down a horrific beast, apparently even more fearsome than sharks

programme.title: Giant Lobster Hunters
programme.desc: Tensions hit boiling point on the William Norling as deckhand Macca makes a monumental blunder that threatens to sink the career of Skipper Danny. Meanwhile, Tasmanian skipper Bryce goes head-to-head with a ferocious storm alone as his deckhand Lachy is sick in bed with a stomach bug.

programme.title: Jaws 2 (1978)
programme.desc: The seaside resort of Amity is terrorised once again by a man-eating great white shark. Thriller sequel, starring Roy Scheider, Lorraine Gary and Murray Hamilton

programme.desc: A 13-year-old aspiring Olympian fractures his pelvis after falling from his bike, an injury that may have devastating long-term consequences for his sporting career. As doctors determine if an operation is needed, his mother reflects on how a spinal injury brought an end to her own dreams when she was a teenager. A motorcyclist is brought in to St Georges, having been flung six feet in the air following a collision with a car

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A beauty queen bricklayer goes looking for love with a bar manager, while two students with very different priorities when it comes to conversation try to make a connection. A financial investigator meets a man who seems to have an answer to everything, and a man whose job takes him all over the world is under pressure to find himself a wife

programme.title: The Undateables
programme.desc: The series following extraordinary singletons as they search for romance returns. Nicholas, a 26-year-old from Berkshire who has Asperger's, longs to find a partner to share his life with. Meanwhile, 22-year-old Shantae, who has Down's Syndrome, goes to the Pentecostal church every Sunday with her family and dreams of meeting a religious family man. Cameras also follow Robbie Williams superfan Sam. He is 28, lives in Devon and has a global learning delay. He's a successful horse rider, competes regularly and has won scores of rosettes. Can he find Ms Right?

programme.title: Undercover Police: Hunting Paedophiles
programme.desc: Following a middle-aged male undercover officer as he assumes a new identity as a 13-year-old girl from Essex and begins engaging online with people who have demonstrated a sexual interest in children. Within minutes of creating an account on a social media site for teenagers, 'Keira' is bombarded with highly sexualised messages from other users, many of whom are adult men

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island. The no-nonsense chef struggles to persuade hot-headed owner Abby to make his suggested changes, and a confrontation results in him being thrown out of the building in the middle of a dinner service. The eatery's co-owner and financier begs Gordon to return, but his success hinges on his ability to win Abby over

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Football pundit Lee Dixon brings his beloved rescue dog to see Professor Noel Fitzpatrick, looking for help treating a limp and getting his pet back to full fitness. Elsewhere, a Springer Spaniel all the way from Guernsey is brought in for spinal surgery, and a Caucasian Shepherd puppy called Bear requires two hip replacements

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: It's Terracotta Week in the pottery, and the potters are tasked to make their own range of cookware. Judge Rich Miller sets a second terracotta challenge to engrave tiles, before he and fellow judge Keith Brymer Jones decide who will be potter of the week and who will be leaving the competition. Siobhan McSweeney hosts

programme.title: Live Test Cricket
programme.desc: India v England. Coverage of the fifth and final day of the Second Test in the four-match series, held at MA Chidambaram Stadium in Chennai. Rishi Persad presents, with analysis from Andrew Strauss and Ebony Rainford-Brent

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Sylver is worried that Mercedes is hiding something from him. Nancy is unsure why Darren is avoiding her, but Jack tells her it is for the best

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back to some of their favourite properties from the show and offers tips and tricks to help viewers make the best of their homes. As well as catching up with some of the families that previously featured, Kirstie and Phil focus on the layout issues and dilemmas that many of us now face in a very different world. In this episode, the duo tackle future-proofing, as two families at very different stages of their lives tackle tricky renovations in a last-ditch attempt to make their homes work for the long term

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: The presenter explores the UK's wildest, most remote and beautifully designed holiday homes, accompanied by some of the nation's most fascinating and funny women. This time, Sandi is joined by BAFTA-winning actor and writer Jessica Hynes to explore the ultimate staycations in Jessica's home county of Kent. The pair visit a lovingly restored early 1900s windmill outside the village of Benenden, before travelling to the Isle of Sheppey, where they explore Elmley National Nature Reserve, the only nature reserve in England where you can stay overnight

programme.title: The Mum Who Got Tourette's
programme.desc: Elizabeth is a typical suburban housewife and mother of three with one big difference - without warning, shortly after her 40th birthday, she developed Tourette's syndrome - a neurological condition that causes involuntary tics, some of them offensive. This one-off documentary follows Elizabeth and her family over the course of a busy summer, including a holiday in Cornwall, GCSE results and meeting her son's new girlfriend - experiences amplified by the unexpected impact of Elizabeth's condition

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 13-year-old aspiring Olympian fractures his pelvis after falling from his bike, an injury that may have devastating long-term consequences for his sporting career. As doctors determine if an operation is needed, his mother reflects on how a spinal injury brought an end to her own dreams when she was a teenager. A motorcyclist is brought in to St Georges, having been flung six feet in the air following a collision with a car

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for the Grasshopper Also in Carlstadt, New Jersey, a family-owned Irish restaurant. There, he lends a helping hand to Mitch Sandler, who has been struggling to live up to the expectations of his father-in-law since he invested heavily in the business. The chef also tries to improve the presentation of dishes emerging from the kitchen

programme.title: Rudderless (2014)
programme.desc: A grieving father in a downward spiral stumbles across a box of his recently deceased son's demo tapes and lyrics. Shocked by the discovery of this unknown talent, he forms a band in the hope of finding some catharsis. Comedy directed by William H. Macy, starring Billy Crudup, Anton Yelchin and Felicity Huffman

programme.title: Fargo
programme.desc: A worried Emmit asks his lawyer to investigate Varga, but an internet search leads to trouble for the attorney. Ray visits his brother in a seeming attempt to make peace with him, but is just creating a diversion while Nikki tries to steal the stamp. Gloria learns that Ennis wrote pulp sci-fi novels under the pseudonym Thaddeus Mobley, and also discovers that Maurice took a page from a phonebook with Ennis's information from the gas station that he stopped in on the night of the murder. Ewan McGregor and Carrie Coon star

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In north Devon, married couple Frankie and Jason kick off the competition. Outspoken duo Kate and Ian don't let the evening's many delays go unnoticed, and as tensions rise around the table, internet lovebirds Chris and Amy try to stay firmly out of the way. Day two sees Kate and Ian serve up a menu full of rustic French cuisine, influenced by their 22 years living in France. But a table laying mishap suggests that Kate has overtly snubbed her guests, although tensions are eased when former falconers Kate and Ian bring out one of their favourite birds of prey. Finally it's dog lovers Chris and Amy's turn to step up to the plate, and believing themselves to be the foodies of the group they're confident they can bag the prize for best soiree

programme.title: Mike & Molly

programme.title: Cheers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Cliff's girlfriend instantly hits it off with his mother, and Rebecca recommends chilled underwear to aid Sam's fertility. Comedy, starring John Ratzenberger, Frances Sternhagen and Margaret O'Keefe

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Doug has nothing to do when his union goes on strike, so he finds a temporary assignment as a substitute teacher at the school where his sister works, but the job is not as easy as he had anticipated. Ricki Lake guest stars

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: The three-week strike sees a miserable Doug and Deacon getting on their wives' nerves. Arthur tries to shake them out of their depression, but his efforts work a little too well as the trio set out to recapture their adolescence. Comedy, starring Kevin James

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphne agrees to marry Donny, but has a vision that suggests he is wrong for her, and tempts fate by turning to Niles for advice on how to proceed

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part one of two. Daphne's engagement to Donny leaves Niles feeling down, so he seeks solace in the arms of a younger woman. Frasier keeps getting his girlfriend's name wrong, Martin's relationship with Bonnie turns sour, and Roz cannot bring herself to break up with Bulldog

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Hairdresser Julie hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham, hoping to win over her guests with a no-nonsense menu she can prepare in advance. Joining her are tough-talking prison transport officer Tina, dreadlocked farm-dweller Stephen, superior IT manager David and glamorous beauty-school teacher Ashleigh. Alliances are quickly forged when David and Tina discover they both love a good gossip, before they turn their attention to Stephen when David compares him to a tramp. Julie overcooks her beef, but will her hosting make up for it and bag her the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty teacher Ashleigh throws the second of the week's parties in Co Durham, she hopes a menu of sophisticated dishes - along with a helping of hearts and love - will help her win the cash prize. However, cracks were already beginning to show in the diners' genial facade yesterday, and the hostess realises she must play peacemaker if she is to keep everyone from falling out

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Call centre complaints officer and part-time farmer Stephen hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Co Durham. He hopes to win the £1,000 prize money by impressing his guests with an evening on his farm, featuring a drinks reception and rustic cuisine. However, self-confessed snob Tina makes it perfectly clear what she thinks of his efforts

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Prison transport officer Tina is the fourth host in Co Durham. She hopes to impress her guests with a sophisticated French menu including salmon crepes, chicken and posh peas and pear dessert. But having been highly critical of everyone else's food so far, will they now take the chance to get their own back or can she win them round?

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson welcomes two more singletons to choose a date after seeing what their potential partners have to offer on a purely physical level. First up is 25-year-old Hope, a model from Birmingham. A huge wrestling fan, she is keen to find a man who can finally pin her down. She is followed by 28-year-old veterinary student Kurt, a real-life Dr Doolittle who can talk to the animals but gets tongue-tied speaking to women. Will Naked Attraction's unique dating method help him overcome his shyness?

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eatery's decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brother's trust before he can save their business

programme.title: Missing or Murdered?
programme.desc: A look at the case of 35-year-old chef Claudia Lawrence, who went missing in 2009 and sparked one of the most high-profile missing persons cases of recent years. The programme examines how media coverage of Claudia's private life impacted on the police investigation, and analyses theories of what really happened, including a recent possible sighting of her in Amsterdam and her mother's fears of sex trafficking

programme.title: Inside
programme.desc: Documentary examining the most famous and feared criminal court in the land, and some of the cases that have been tried there, including Doctor Crippen and John Christie. The history of the building itself is also assessed, from the creation of the first Old Bailey in 1539 to its iconic facelift in 1907

programme.title: The Butcher Surgeon: Why Wasn't He Stopped?
programme.desc: Documentary profiling former breast surgeon Ian Paterson, who was recently convicted of intentionally wounding patients by performing needless operations for more than a decade. Paterson was found guilty on 17 counts, and this programme examines how was he able to continue for so long, asking whether his employers might have ignored or failed to act on a series of warnings stretching back to 2003. Some of Paterson's victims are also interviewed, along with health service whistleblowers

programme.desc: Experienced enforcement agent Stewart and trainee Iain head to Blackpool to chase down a builder owing nearly £3,500 to an unhappy customer, while father and son Del and Dael are in Essex to collect over £2,500 owed to a frozen food company. In south London, Del and Brian attempt to recover nearly £10,000 from a debtor who acted as guarantor for a friend's rental agreement, ending in a bizarre stand-off with the debtor's daughter

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime

programme.title: Build a New Life in the Country
programme.desc: George Clarke helps two IT managers move to the village of Gweek in Cornwall, advising them as they renovate a dilapidated houseboat and attempt to begin a business ferrying divers to wrecks around the coast. The four-month deadline seems hopelessly tight as the need for new plumbing delays other work, and the fledgling venture has difficulty getting off the ground so late in the season

programme.title: Home and Away

programme.desc: Single mother Jessica's warm relationship with her 18-year-old daughter Tessa is thrown into turmoil when the teenager starts dating an older man against her wishes. The crisis becomes toxic when Jessica realises the older man is the person who attacked her when she was in college - and it seems revenge is his main motive. Thriller, starring Clare Kramer, Mia Topalian and David Fumero

programme.title: Dogs Behaving (Very) Badly
programme.desc: Graeme Hall treats a ridgeback-boxer cross that has become aggressive towards other dogs after being attacked, and helps a cancer patient with a badly behaved golden retriever that is holding back her recovery. His last port of call is to a miniature pincher who objects to any men coming near its owner

Eviction expert Paul helps Allen gain possession of his flat when his tenant refuses to move out, and letting agent Paul faces a sticky situation when presented with a guarantor who has been stitched up by his son, who is in turn refusing to pay rent

programme.title: Inside
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the secret of the supermarket chain's success. The programme examines the launch of a brand of organic food from Prince Charles' own duchy and reveals how the opening of a new Waitrose store has been known to boost house prices in the surrounding area. A taste test is performed to see how Waitrose's own brands compare to their rivals

programme.title: Secrets of the Luxury Super Yachts
programme.desc: Documentary following the 'birth' of a new, 65-metre luxury vessel called Life Saga - designed, sold and captained by the best of British boaters. This expose of the secretive world of super yachts also spills the beans on how the super-rich really behave on board their boats, reveals how security teams employ hi-tech tools to defend against pirates, and features the brokers who shake hands on mega-million pound deals

programme.title: Sally Lindsay's Posh Sleepover
programme.desc: The actress is a house guest at upmarket homes around the UK, looking around the five-star residences and getting a taste of the owners' luxury lifestyles. For her first sleepover, Sally heads to central London where her host is property tycoon Kam and partner Anya, who live in a Mayfair townhouse worth over £30 million and not far from Buckingham Palace

programme.title: Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, Del and Dael Anglin receive a hostile reception from the brother of a debtor who has alreasy defaulted on a promise to pay £2,000 she owes to a storage company. In Worcestershire, Gareth and Craig try to recover £45,000 from a young online entrepreneur, who claims no responsibility for the debt he owes to his courier. Their target cooks up a flimsy lie in an effort to absolve himself, but dishonesty proves to be the least of the agents' worries when they discover a stash of guns at the back of the garage. In east London, an Indian restaurant owner begins to take his financial responsibilities seriously when Del and Dael start removing cookers and freezers from his premises, and a pair of pensioners in Oxfordshire hope the High Court will provide them a lifeline as they try to pay off a £4,000 bill to a solicitors' firm after a failed compensation claim

With a wedding due in a few weeks, the completion date looms ominously

Thriller, starring Bree Williamson

programme.title: Gardening with Carol Klein
programme.desc: First of a two-part masterclass in which Carol Klein shares her gardening knowledge, covering everything from putting the right plant in the right place and growing plants from scratch to achieving edibles and getting beautiful results from ornamentals. She begins by discussing the importance of observing nature, to understand how plants grow in the wild, and how a gardener can work out the conditions of their specific plot

programme.title: This Week on the Farm
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Jules Hudson follow the daily lives of farming brothers Rob and Dave Nicholson at Cannon Hall Farm in South Yorkshire. It's a race against time for Rob and Dave as they try to catch their wayward cockerels before chicken lockdown commences due to an outbreak of avian flu. JB Gill visits a dairy farm in Lincolnshire to meet the cows who live a life of luxury and produce milk to match

programme.title: Wartime Britain
programme.desc: Part two of two. A team of experts and historians brings the reality of the home front to life as a family experiences what life was like during the Second World War. The guinea pigs cook up ration recipes, learn the jitterbug, discover tricks to make do and mend, while archive footage and interviews are used to chart the course of war on the home front

programme.title: Esther Rantzen: Living with Grief
programme.desc: Campaigner and presenter Esther Rantzen sets out to explore the experience of bereavement and coping with grief. It's exactly 20 years since the death of Esther's husband, the broadcaster Desmond Wilcox, but in many ways she has yet to come to terms with her loss. Here she enlists the help of six individuals who each have their own story to tell, from a woman who lost her husband to Covid-19 and a mother whose child drowned, to a young man who was just 11 when his brother was murdered

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: Retired coach driver Paul is rushed in after falling onto fire-stoking tools, while a 21-year-old Harris is brought in by police after allegedly being attacked by someone with a machete. After being thrown from his motorbike in a road traffic collision, 19-year-old Callum arrives in resus by ambulance, while nine-year-old Kayden is in the paediatrics department with a broken leg after falling off his scooter

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed

programme.title: Eamonn & Ruth: How the Other Half Lives
programme.desc: Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford meet the who's who of Britain's rich list to work out their secret rules. Jacqueline Gold, the extraordinary force behind Ann Summers, welcomes them into her luxury home to explain the importance of having a revolutionary idea. She also teaches Ruth the ins-and-outs of hosting the perfect sex toy party. Then duo then meet millionaire philanthropist Jack Petchey, still working hard at the age of 91, to learn how much of the secret is simply hard graft. Plus, the man who claims to be the highest paid life coach in the UK offers some advice

programme.title: OMG
programme.desc: Cases include Ryan, who has spent more than £40,000 on his groomed appearance, though his risky tanning injections are damaging his relationship. Leza is obsessed with mirroring the girls she sees online, Brigid has surgically sculpted the perfect selfie body, while Jema is an old-school glamour girl who hates the new breed of Instagram models

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: Documentary about the work of dedicated animal lovers who save injured and orphaned wild creatures

programme.title: House Busters
programme.desc: Janet Ellis and her team of new-age practitioners suggest ways in which changes to homes could positively affect the lives of their owners

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Property expert Ann Maurice suggests improvements for a four-bedroom dormer bungalow in Clapham, West Sussex, to make it more appealing to prospective buyers. The lurid wall colours and pink living room carpet are banished, leaving pine floorboards that help create a sense of the outdoors

programme.title: The Violent Men (1955)
programme.desc: A ruthless cattle baron sets out to create his own private empire, no matter what the cost. When he resorts to murder, he makes an enemy of a civil war veteran, while his lust for power drives his wife into the arms of his younger brother. Western, starring Edward G Robinson, Barbara Stanwyck and Glenn Ford

programme.title: 40 Guns to Apache Pass (1967)
programme.desc: A cavalry captain sets out to deliver a shipment of rifles to a fort besieged by marauding Apaches, but the mission is undermined by a villainous corporal who plans to steal the weapons and sell them to the enemy. Western, starring Audie Murphy, Michael Burns, Laraine Stephens, Kenneth Tobey and Michael Keep

programme.title: The Court-Martial of Billy Mitchell (1955)
programme.desc: In the 1920s, an American colonel accuses the war department of criminal negligence for denying the air force the funding it needs - but the powers-that-be still regard aircraft as an irrelevant part of the military, and respond by putting him on trial. Courtroom drama, starring Gary Cooper and Rod Steiger, with Ralph Bellamy and Elizabeth Montgomery

programme.title: The Last Frontier (1955)
programme.desc: A backwoods trapper signs up as a cavalry scout at a fort manned by inexperienced soldiers, where he falls in love with a colonel's wife. On taking over command of the garrison, the officer plans to lead his men in a reckless attack on a hostile Indian tribe - and it is down to the frontiersman to save his comrades from a massacre. Western, starring Victor Mature, Robert Preston and Anne Bancroft

programme.title: Film4 Interview Programmes
programme.desc: Stars and directors talk about the experience of making movies in this compendium of personal and practical reflections

programme.title: Home Again (2017)
programme.desc: Five months after separating from her husband, Alice Kinney and her daughters relocate from New York to her childhood home in Los Angeles, which belonged to her late father, Oscar-winning film director John Kinney. Eventually things take an unexpected turn when she lets three young guys move in with her. Romantic comedy, starring Reese Witherspoon, Michael Sheen and Candice Bergen

programme.title: Hunter Killer (2018)
programme.desc: Unconventional submarine skipper Joe Glass is sent to investigate the torpedoing of US and Russian vessels. He winds up trying to rescue the Russian president, who has been kidnapped by a rogue general. Action thriller, starring Gerard Butler, Gary Oldman and Common

programme.title: Blue Is the Warmest Colour (2013)
programme.desc: A 15-year-old girl falls in love with an older woman and tentatively begins a relationship. They remain together for years to come, as the teenager grows to adulthood and is inspired by her partner to explore her desires and develop her own outlook on the world. Romantic drama, with Lea Seydoux and Adele Exarchopoulos. In French and English

programme.title: Hondo (1953)
programme.desc: A hard-boiled cavalry scout with a feral dog as companion stumbles across a woman and her child stranded on their homestead deep inside hostile territory. Despite repeated attacks by the Apaches, they refuse to leave their home, leaving the scout with no choice but to stay and defend her. Western, starring John Wayne, Geraldine Page, Ward Bond and Michael Pate

programme.title: The Day Will Dawn (1942)
programme.desc: A foreign correspondent working in occupied Norway is assigned to an RAF detail helping freedom fighters to sabotage an important German U-boat installation. He enlists the help of a local sea captain's daughter, but the plan goes wrong and he ends up in enemy hands, facing death by firing squad. Second World War adventure, with Hugh Williams, Deborah Kerr and Ralph Richardson

programme.title: Kidnapped (1971)
programme.desc: A naive young man is cheated out of his inheritance by his conniving uncle, who sells him to the captain of a slave ship. On board he forms an alliance with a hardened rebel, and the pair make their escape when the ship is wrecked on the Scottish coast. Period adventure based on Robert Louis Stevenson's novel set during the Jacobite Uprising, with Michael Caine, Lawrence Douglas, Trevor Howard, Jack Hawkins and Donald Pleasence

programme.title: Gun Fury (1953)
programme.desc: A wealthy rancher and his fiancee take the stagecoach to California, where they intend to start a new life in the wake of the Civil War. However, during the journey an ex-Confederate outlaw and his gang of thugs attack the coach and kidnap the bride-to-be. Distraught and wounded, the rancher sets out to rescue her and punish her abductors. Western, with Rock Hudson, Donna Reed, Lee Marvin and Phil Carey

programme.title: Everest (2015)
programme.desc: Two expeditions set out to climb to the summit of the world's highest mountain. Both parties of mountaineers get caught in one of the fiercest snowstorms ever recorded, and face a desperate struggle to survive against the elements. Fact-based adventure, starring Jason Clarke, Josh Brolin, Jake Gyllenhaal and Keira Knightley

programme.title: Roman J Israel, Esq (2018)
programme.desc: A driven, idealistic defence attorney gets mixed up in a series of events that lead to a crisis and the necessity for extreme action. Crime drama, starring Denzel Washington, Colin Farrell and Carmen Ejogo

programme.title: Beautiful Thing (1996)
programme.desc: A tentative relationship develops between two very different gay teenagers - Jamie, a shy outsider living with his brassy single mother, and Ste, who is popular at school but regularly on the receiving end of violence from his father and brother at home. Coming-of-age drama, starring Glen Berry, Scott Neal, Linda Henry, Meera Syal and Ben Daniels

programme.title: Everybody Wants Some!! (2016)
programme.desc: College baseball players enjoy a raucous weekend while preparing to start university in 1980. Richard Linklater's comedy, starring Blake Jenner and Juston Street

programme.title: The Professionals (1966)
programme.desc: A wealthy Texan hires four mercenaries to rescue his wife, who has been abducted and taken to Mexico by a bandit gang. However, when they find her, she refuses to leave and they have to take her by force - ending in a cross-border chase with the outlaws on their trail. Western adventure, with Burt Lancaster, Lee Marvin, Robert Ryan, Woody Strode, Jack Palance and Claudia Cardinale

programme.title: The Great Sioux Massacre (1965)
programme.desc: A corrupt senator coaxes Colonel Custer out of retirement with the prospect of presidential office, and the soldier tries to further his political ambitions by wresting control of the current military campaign from its commanding officer, culminating in his last stand at the Battle of the Little Bighorn. Western, with Philip Carey, Joseph Cotten, Darren McGavin and Julie Sommars

programme.title: Tora! Tora! Tora! (1970)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War drama recounting the bombing of Pearl Harbour from both the Japanese and American perspectives, reconstructing the events leading up to the devastating attack on the US naval base in 1941. Starring Martin Balsam, So Yamamura, Joseph Cotten, James Whitmore and Jason Robards

programme.title: Now You See Me 2 (2016)
programme.desc: A gang of magicians-turned-criminals is reunited for a comeback performance by a ruthless technology magnate, whose actual goal is to force the team into carrying out a seemingly impossible heist on his behalf. The illusionists realise their only hope of avoiding the law is to perform one last unprecedented stunt to expose the businessman's illegal activities. Crime thriller sequel, starring Jesse Eisenberg, Mark Ruffalo, Daniel Radcliffe and Morgan Freeman

programme.title: Valkyrie (2008)
programme.desc: Fact-based thriller, recreating the 1944 plot to assassinate Adolf Hitler using a briefcase bomb. As the tide of the Second World War turns in favour of the Allies, a group of senior German officers recruits war hero Colonel Claus von Stauffenberg to spearhead an attempt on the Fuehrer's life. Starring Tom Cruise, Kenneth Branagh, Bill Nighy and Tom Wilkinson

programme.title: Alone in Berlin (2016)
programme.desc: After a Nazi Party-supporting German working-class couple lose their son during the Second World War, they decide to retaliate by secretly distributing handwritten cards in Berlin denouncing their government. Drama, starring Emma Thompson, Brendan Gleeson and Daniel Bruhl

programme.title: Mr & Mrs Smith (2005)
programme.desc: An outwardly bored suburban couple lead double lives as professional assassins, both unaware of their partner's secret. When they are hired to kill each other, the deadly situation puts some much-needed excitement back into their stale relationship. Comedy adventure, starring Brad Pitt, Angelina Jolie, Vince Vaughn and Adam Brody

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits four restaurants he earlier tried to save, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or fell by the wayside. First he visits the Spanish Pavillion in New Jersey, then heads to California to see the owners of Kingston Cafe, French-themed La Frite, and Capri

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: Harry Hill hosts as the remaining 10 contestants begin finals week, making caramel in the technical challenge and creating chocolate cakes in the showstopper

programme.title: Malcolm in the Middle
programme.desc: Francis encourages Lois to let him come home following his self-emancipation, while Malcolm and the Krelboynes do battle with a difficult new teacher. Family comedy, starring Bryan Cranston, Christopher Kennedy Masterson and Jane Kaczmarek

programme.title: Malcolm in the Middle
programme.desc: Lois joins a book club to escape the stresses of her home life. Meanwhile, Hal frets about how to make the boys behave in her absence, and Francis hitch-hikes through Canada. Comedy, starring Frankie Muniz, Bryan Cranston and Jane Kaczmarek

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon discovers a surprise screening of The Avengers is scheduled at a local cinema, complete with a Q&A session with the film's director, Joss Whedon. However, the event just happens to clash with time he'd planned to spend with Amy, so he decides to pay Stuart to be his subtitute for the afternoon while he and the other boys immerse themselves in Marvel superheroics. Meanwhile, Leonard's mother Beverly arrives in town for an extended visit, and Penny takes the opportunity to bond with her new mother-in-law - and to make up for not inviting her to the wedding. Comedy, guest starring Christine Baranski

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Conflict arises when Leonard's newly divorced parents and Sheldon's devoutly religious mother are all in town at the same time. And when Howard and Raj are contacted about the guidance system they've developed, they assume that the government is out to get them. Laurie Metcalf, Christine Baranski and Judd Hirsch guest star

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly is pitted against a rival mother when she tries to micro-manage Barry and Erica's search for college places, while Erica tells Pops that she has tried to enhance her prospects by lying about her extracurricular activities. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam plans to attend the school dance with Dana, and reluctantly agrees to take dancing lessons with Beverly. Meanwhile, Murray is forced to have the 'talk' with Adam, but he admits that he hasn't spoken to Barry about sex yet. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Diaz begs Peralta to help her break some big news to her parents, but her plan is thrown off track when Mr and Mrs Diaz wrongly assume that their daughter and her colleague are dating. The squad suffers internet problems after the Cyber Crimes Unit move into the office downstairs

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Gangster Seamus Murphy asks Holt to pay back the favour he owes for getting Diaz and Peralta out of jail, so the cops try to help the captain keep his end of the bargain without breaking the law

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Stuart takes Penny out for a second date, during which she drunkenly reveals her true feelings. Meanwhile, Howard uncovers a flaw in the toilet he designed for a Nasa space shuttle, and asks his friends to help him correct it before the launch. Kaley Cuoco, Kevin Sussman and Simon Helberg star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon wins a grant to pay for a summer expedition to the North Pole, and invites his friends to join him. However, the gang's imminent departure causes Leonard and Penny to reconsider their feelings for each other. Johnny Gale and Kaley Cuoco star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gang goes to a bar and runs into Penny's old boyfriend Zack, while Bernadette is left to spend the evening with Sheldon when she cannot go wine tasting with the others. Elsewhere, things do not go to plan when Raj introduces his new girlfriend, Claire. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray gets his moment in the spotlight when he is honoured by his old high school for his basketball prowess. While he and Beverly are away from home, Barry takes advantage of their absence to throw a party to impress his school friends

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam tries to express his feelings for Dana by making her a mix tape, but disaster strikes when the tape falls into Beverly's hands. Barry obtains a fake ID and takes it to school to show off to his classmates. David Spade (Rules of Engagement) guest stars, with Sean Giambrone, Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The squad members worry about Diaz when she responds to an active shooter alert, but are instructed to stay back at the precinct building and are powerless to help. Peralta and Boyle try to find ways to assist Diaz, Jeffords has an existential crisis, while Santiago and Gina attempt to repair the broken toilet in the ladies' room

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Diaz and Santiago team up to take down a ruthless killer who has managed to elude them for seven years. Jeffords helps Peralta to tackle the chores on his wedding planning list. Captain Holt and his rival Olivia Crawford try to convince each other to step down from the campaign to become Police Commissioner

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: As the experiment continues, things get wild during a night out where scandalous secrets are revealed and gossip leads to a confrontation that threatens to change everything

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: The competition starts at the Cimon in Torquay, Devon, where owners Mark and Jo Warden hope their four-star establishment will impress their guests

programme.desc: The second visit is to The Cardinal's Hat in Worcester, where owner Nigel Smith has decades of hospitality experience, but Mark and Jo are concerned by the condition of the bedding

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Third to host this week are husband and wife duo Russ and Karen Colman, who hope their glamping site in Cledan Valley, mid-Wales, will win over their rivals

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Lucinda Renshaw welcomes the guests to Springfield House B&B in Instow, Devon, where things get off to a good start - until one couple realise they do not have a lock on their door

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. The Cimon is first and hosts Mark and Jo want to question Karen and Russ about their breakfast feedback. Next, Nigel from The Cardinal's Hat wants clarity on some cleaning and hygiene comments made by Mark and Jo. Russ and Karen from Cledan Valley are third and are keen to put Mark and Jo straight. Finally, it's Springfield House and owner Lucinda and friend Diane want to put two of the guests at ease, but it's the payments where true feelings are expressed

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets Ian and Becky from Lincolnshire, who are embarking on the restoration of a rare VW Phoenix campervan. But this couple are not doing things by halves, having decided to add a sound-activated dance floor to the vehicle. He also visits Stirlingshire, where a small family business plans to convert a Sea King helicopter into luxury overnight accommodation, and travels to an ordinary residential street on the outskirts of Auckland, New Zealand, to see a dome home that is out of this world. Plus, George and Will design a front door for their rotating house

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: African painted dogs K'mana and Ville were thrown together as a breeding couple after the dominant female died, but K'mana was never Ville's first choice as partner and still carries a limp from her battles with Ville's previous mate. Now she is pregnant with the zoo's first ever litter of this rare and endangered species. Meanwhile, keepers try to rekindle interest between Okapi pair Dicky and Stuma, and Atlas moths Dave and Simon only have five days of life after hatching, and their sole purpose is to mate

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: In northern France, Wendy and Marcus start renovating their chateau's barn into a space fit for a wedding venue. Debbie and Simon test run a possible new business venture by hosting a Chinese cooking weekend for their visiting family. Meanwhile, in a bid to top up their renovation funds, Belinda and Lee plan to host a fashion show by their swimming pool, but find wet weather threatens to stop play

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits Robert and Milla Gaukroger who, when he last saw them, were in danger of their bank foreclosing on their unfinished timber house with views of Lake Windermere. They had begun construction with only a quarter of the £400,000 budget needed, and the physical effort of turning the 1980s property into an eco-home that mirrored the surrounding mountains had begun to put Robert's health at risk

programme.title: The Great British Dig: History in Your Back Garden
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis is joined by a team of expert archaeologists to excavate back gardens around Britain, uncovering the lost history buried beneath our lawns and flower beds. In the first episode, Hugh and the team attempt to uncover a Roman fort, connected to Hadrian's Wall, that is buried beneath the back gardens of the quiet suburban streets of Benwell in Newcastle

programme.title: Jon Snow's Very Hard Questions
programme.desc: Quiz show where teams from across the UK go head to head as they face tough questions on topics including science, sport and history. A collection of clues are available to help the teams find the answers, but using them comes with a cost - the more clues they use, the more points they lose. In the first episode, Intelli-Gents and Assassins' Guild compete. Hosted by Jon Snow

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Sean Lock and Harry Hill take on Jon Richardson and Rose Matafeo, while Alex Horne and the Horne Section join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Test Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: India v England. Rishi Persad presents action from day five of the Second Test in the four-match series, held at MA Chidambaram Stadium in Chennai

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A man has a blackout driving a van and crashes into a telegraph pole. This is the second time it has happened in as many weeks and paramedics are keen to find out why

programme.title: The Great British Dig: History in Your Back Garden
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis is joined by a team of expert archaeologists to excavate back gardens around Britain, uncovering the lost history buried beneath our lawns and flower beds. In the first episode, Hugh and the team attempt to uncover a Roman fort, connected to Hadrian's Wall, that is buried beneath the back gardens of the quiet suburban streets of Benwell in Newcastle

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Mrs Doyle wins a competition to meet a famous singer, and discreetly invites him round for tea - but the secret slips out, and soon the house is besieged by hordes of adoring pensioners. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Pauline McLynn

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton visits the world's largest jelly bean producers to discover what they are made of instead of gelatine, and Matt Tebbutt finds out why maple syrup is so expensive

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas is property-searching around Naples, one of the most stylish cities along Florida's Gulf Coast, for house-hunters Trever and Reiss

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien come across a gramophone and a stow-away bed to restore. Meanwhile, Simon accidently puts his foot through a drum

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole decides that he needs to restore a forklift truck, while Simon O'Brien gets all zen with a stone water feature

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: In northern France, Wendy and Marcus start renovating their chateau's barn into a space fit for a wedding venue. Debbie and Simon test run a possible new business venture by hosting a Chinese cooking weekend for their visiting family. Meanwhile, in a bid to top up their renovation funds, Belinda and Lee plan to host a fashion show by their swimming pool, but find wet weather threatens to stop play

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps Cheshire architect Matthew Lewis, who is trying to build the perfect home for a very demanding client - Hayley, his wife. Hayley, a GP, has found an extraordinary rural site on the edge of the Peak District, and Matthew plans to use his own building firm to create a contemporary single-storey family home that makes the most of the stunning views. To make ends meet, the couple have been living in a caravan and the pressure is on Matthew to finally put a proper roof over the family's head

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the Edge Accommodation in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, where owner John Whitworth is hoping to win over his guests with his unique style and personality

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Darwin Lake Holiday Village in Matlock, Derbyshire, managed by Sally McAnulty and Teresa Shirley, who pride themselves on their exclusive village feel

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The group head to Sands Resort Hotel and Spa in Newquay, where father-and-son team Nicholas and Tom Malcolm pride themselves on the vast facilities they have on offer

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Phil Gilbert's Oak Tree Lane campsite in Bratton Clovelly, Devon, where the guests are wowed by the traditional yurts, only for arguments to cause tension

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. The Edge Accommodation is first, and host John wants to shut the door on some criticism. Next, Sally and Teresa want to challenge John over his comments on their hosting skills

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George travels to Portland in Dorset where a couple are building a shipwreck-themed garden room with unrestricted views over Chesil Beach. In south London, he meets a woman converting a 1980s campervan into a mobile canine holiday home, and in New Zealand he travels to a remote lodge made almost entirely of glass. He also looks for inspiration on how to put a garden inside his rotating home

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pregnant giraffe Orla goes into labour, but the keepers must watch from the sidelines due to the risks involved during the birth. Baby Bornean orang-utan Tombol tries his luck with the gibbons as he heads out and explores his new habitat. Elsewhere, northern bald ibis chick Professor X is proving too confident for his own good, and when it is time to release him into the adult habitat he gets into troubled waters and suffers a nasty head injury, so the vets have to work hard to help him pull through

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Wendy and Marcus aim to finish their newly renovated wedding barn for a VIP visit from the local mayor. In the Ardèche, chateau owners Amy and Marc have big plans to turn two of their outbuildings into a yoga studio and café to kick-start their fledgling business of running a restorative retreat centre. Meanwhile, Belinda and Lee's hopes of giving their donjon a much-needed facelift start to fade as a concrete delivery fails to work upon arrival

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits an arch-shaped home made of clay tiles in the Weald of Kent, built by Richard Hawkes, who designed the property, and his wife Sophie. It was intended to be energy self-sufficient and able to supply surplus power to the National Grid, but serious problems occurred when the arch collapsed during construction. The presenter returns to see if their innovative home has lived up to its promise

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: The idyllic village of Hartley Wintney is often voted one of the best places to live in Britain - but every rose has its thorn. Fiona and Hugh's ugly Hampshire home has not been touched in decades, and they hate absolutely everything except the garden. They have £180,000 budget to transform the entire house, so George enlists award-winning architect Greg Blee. However, while the owners buy into his modernist scheme the local planners do not. Can he salvage his design and come up with a scheme that will tick all the boxes?

programme.title: The Tudors
programme.desc: Drama series about Henry VIII's 39-year reign. The king puts Cardinal Wolsey in charge of the war effort against the hated French, but the clergyman's personal agenda leads him to orchestrate talks in preparation for an ambitious peace treaty instead. Jonathan Rhys Meyers, Sam Neill and Jeremy Northam star

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Schoolboy Eddie, 12, is brought into A&E having been hit by a car on his way home from school, and doctors are concerned he may have suffered a bleed on the brain, as well as damage to his kidneys. Meanwhile, 64-year-old Linda, who breathes with the help of a tracheostomy, arrives at King's after experiencing respiratory failure at home, and student Amy, 20, has been coughing up blood

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy game show, with Sean Lock and Greg Davies taking on Jon Richardson and Holly Walsh. Susie Dent is joined by Vic Reeves in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: New Amsterdam
programme.desc: Sharpe discovers a long-kept secret regarding a patient, leading to trouble for Max and the board. Kapoor introduces a patient to Iggy's PTSD group

programme.title: Father Ted

programme.desc: A SWAT Team from Miramar, Florida, executes a search warrant on a dangerous drug dealer, and Custom and Border Protection Officers in Miami seize an alarming amount of drugs

programme.title: Drug Wars

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May set out to discover if they can go motor racing for less than it costs to play golf. There's also a double helping of guests, with rock star Slash and Formula One driver Kimi Raikkonen taking to the track in Reasonably Priced Cars. Richard also gets behind the wheel of the BMW M5, which is powered by a twin-turbo V8 engine instead of the high-revving V10 of its predecessor

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson attempts to build a car that is even smaller than the Peel P50 and then tests it out on the streets of London before pitching his concept in the Dragons' Den. James May puts the Bentley Continental GT Speed through its paces by taking it on a rally stage, while Richard Hammond drives the new Pagani Huayra hypercar before handing it over to the Stig. Actor Damian Lewis goes for a spin in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Spain
programme.desc: The chef continues his culinary tour in Castile - a region famous not only for its vast plains and castles but also as the home of 11th-century military leader El Cid - before heading to La Mancha, an area renowned for its rustic dishes and wine production, as well as being the land of Miguel de Cervantes' literary character Don Quixote

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The trio of motoring enthusiasts embark on another epic road trip, this time across western America in front-engined supercars. Jeremy Clarkson pilots the Lexus LFA, which has a spectacularly high-revving V10 powerplant, Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the muscular Dodge Viper, and James May opts for the latest incarnation of Aston Martin's Vanquish. On their journey from Las Vegas to Palm Springs via Los Angeles, the presenters tackle racing circuits and find themselves in a race against the police, before rushing to the Mexican border - and there's a terrifying penalty for the last man to arrive. Meanwhile, Fleetwood Mac drummer Mick Fleetwood rolls up his sleeves to thrash the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a race from London's Wembley Stadium to Milan's San Siro, the home of Italian football giants AC and Inter. A price restriction leads Jeremy to grab the keys to a Shelby Mustang GT500, which costs the equivalent of £35,000 in America and packs a supercharged punch of more than 650bhp. James and Richard pin their hopes on the pan-European rail network in a bid to secure the prize of tickets to a top-flight football match, with nothing but a long journey home awaiting the losers. On the test track, Jeremy puts Toyota's GT86 coupe through its paces, and its promise of entertaining rear-wheel-drive handling should put a smile on his face. Plus, singer-songwriter Amy Macdonald gets to grips with the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets tasks for Roisin Conaty, Tim Key, Romesh Ranganathan, Frank Skinner and Josh Widdicombe. They have to identify the contents of a table full of mystery pies, make a film backwards and high five a 55 year old. Alex Horne helps coax the contestants to reveal how comic, creative and seriously competitive they are

Plus, in Nogales Arizona, law enforcement works round the clock to counter traffickers' endlessly inventive techniques for smuggling drugs. Custom and Border Protection officers take apart one particularly suspicious vehicle that may be hiding drugs in a compartment that's extremely difficult to access

programme.title: Drug Wars
programme.desc: Custom and Border Protection officers in the Port of Miami and on the legendary Miami River are on high alert over a resurgence of cocaine smuggling

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson puts the Kia Cee'd through a rigorous but peculiar road test featuring eels, curry, a rock legend, an American police officer and an unusual game of rugby involving James May. Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the Mastretta MXT, but worries about the repercussions of a comment he made in a previous episode about Mexico, the sports car's country of manufacture. Plus, three high-performance hatchbacks are driven with gusto around the test track, and Formula One driver Lewis Hamilton gets a second chance to smash the lap record

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond try to solve the problems that increasingly complex cars pose to an ageing population. They design a vehicle for elderly people and put it to the test in Dorset with the help of three pensioners. Meanwhile, James May gets acquainted with a revamped Range Rover in London before pitting the luxurious off-roader against an autonomous military machine in Nevada. Actor James McAvoy tackles the test track in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a series of extended culinary breaks, visiting markets, restaurants, wineries, cafes and bars, looking for food excellence and also some innovative recipes that will encourage viewers to cook at home. He begins his first adventure in autumnal Bordeaux, where he helps judge a local wine contest and hires a 2CV for an excursion to the coast - abundant with mussels and oysters. At home, he cooks a memorable dish of steak frites with Bordelaise sauce

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May immerse themselves in nostalgia and try to prove that hot hatches from their younger days are better than today's equivalents. Jeremy grabs the keys to a Volkswagen Golf GTI, Richard takes the wheel of a Vauxhall Nova SRi and James May opts for the Ford Fiesta XR2i, and the trio set off on a road trip that includes a hill climb and a visit to a supermarket. However, their adventure concludes at an army training ground in Wales, where they and their steeds must face the heavily armed Top Gear Police Department. Downton Abbey star Hugh Bonneville blasts the Reasonably Priced Car around the test track

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the mid-engined Alfa Romeo 4C on the shores of Lake Como in Italy - but his enjoyment of the lightweight vehicle is disturbed by the arrival of Jeremy Clarkson, who demands a race against his quad bike. Jeremy also visits Belgium, putting the McLaren P1 hypercar to the test in Bruges and around the Spa-Francorchamps Formula One circuit - and its combined power output of more than 900bhp generated from electricity and turbocharged petrol V8 power is likely to cause astonishment. Meanwhile, James May spends time at Camp Bastion in Afghanistan to learn about the army's vehicle redeployment efforts, and Thor star Tom Hiddleston tries to coax the best time he can muster from the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets challenges for Roisin Conaty, Tim Key, Romesh Ranganathan, Frank Skinner and Josh Widdicombe. They have to fill an egg cup with tears, remember an Aussie rules football team and play Taskmaster squash

programme.desc: Wayne sells a 2003 BMW Z8 Alpina roadster and a cute 1958 BMW Isetta 300. Plus, he falls for a vintage 1962 Vespa scooter restored for fashion designer Ralph Lauren

programme.desc: Mike Brewer tries to double his money by working with expert mechanic Edd China to turn an Austin-Healey Frogeye Sprite into a quality classic car. Once completed, the duo test-drive it around the Brooklands Motor Museum in Surrey, prior to selling it on at a profit
