programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies is on Spain's Costa Blanca house-hunting around the vibrant resort of Villamartin with Amanda and Steve, who want to buy a holiday home where they can spend the winter months golfing, socialising and soaking up the sun. Danni has been given a budget of £85,000, with which she finds five properties that she thinks fits the bill. Will Amanda and Steve love one of them sufficiently to buy it?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: A recently married couple seek to mark the next chapter of their lives by buying a holiday apartment together in Fuerteventura, one of Spain's Canary Islands. Jean and Stuart Knight are planning to spend several weeks there every year both on their own and also with family and friends. Ben Hillman shows the pair five options within their budget of £60,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps former firefighter Gary Sheen and his partner Kevin Lambert from Essex find a new home in the Altea region of Spain's Costa Blanca. With a budget of £175,000, the couple are torn between a two-bedroom apartment with jaw-dropping views, or a town house opposite one of their favourite restaurants

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first night in Peterborough, 39-year-old land surveyor and part-time musician Muneer hopes to win over his guests with a menu based on Pakistani recipes passed down through his family for generations. The authenticity extends beyond his food, with Muneer seating his guests on the floor of his specially dressed dining room. His guests include driving instructor Christine, hairdresser Ben, fiery Brazilian Rosie and fridge salesman Gregory. While his food receives mixed reviews, His guests discover his master plan for musical success, suffer bad jokes and find out that one of their fellow diners believes in mermaids

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Peterborough, driving instructor Christine hopes her vegetarian cuisine will grab her the £1,000 prize. But she's worried about being in the firing line, after Rosie compared Muneer's food to vomit on the first night. Now Moony's determined to give Rosie a public dressing down. However, with everyone dreading Christine's meat-free and bland-sounding menu, Rosie may not be the only one who has to bite her lip

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Peterborough, mobile hairdresser Ben plans a gourmet night. Most of his guests are looking forward to some meat after Christine's vegetarian feast the night before. And after laid-back Muneer and critical Rosie seem to have put their tension behind them at Christine's, Ben's determined to take things up a notch as he entertains his guests with his amazing body art, cooking skills and pet crabs

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fridge salesman Gregory serves up a Spanish menu in Peterborough, accompanied by a charm offensive that he hopes will win him the £1,000 prize. Will it work or will the guests give him the cold shoulder?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fiery Brazilian Rosie is confident that her night will be the best of the week in Peterborough. After Gregory used packet mixes, Rosie is determined to show him how to throw a dinner party by making everything from scratch. She's ruffled a few feathers throughout the week with her brutally honest critiques, and her guests, roused for retribution, scour every element of her Brazilian night for any faults, from her samba dancers to her authentic pig-ear stew

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Nashville, Tennessee, the spiritual home of country music, but his visit begins on a sour note at Chappy's Cajun restaurant. Chef John Chapman's careless methods have damaged his reputation, but he blames the local clientele rather than his less-than-appetising food and disturbing kitchen practices. Can Gordon get through to this stubborn owner and save the eatery from disaster?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps John and Soussan search for a holiday home on Spain's Costa Calida. The pair hope to find an apartment with a pool for when their grandchildren come to visit

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Mariam and Nasser, from the West Midlands, find a family holiday home they can eventually retire to in the countryside of Murcia, south-east Spain. The couple used to live near Madrid and desperately miss the Spanish sunshine and way of life, especially the outdoor cooking. Laura has five sizzling hot properties to show them for their £70,000 budget

programme.title: Mend It for Money
programme.desc: A classic 1980s Volkswagen Golf Mk1, a creepy-looking early 20th-century child's highchair, and a side cabinet are brought in for restoration

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: George looks back at his first ever big build, in which he enlisted the help of friend and design guru Will Hardie to convert a dilapidated 1970s caravan bought for £300 into a stylish holiday home for him and his children. Along the way, George takes inspiration from other ingenious caravan and self-build conversions, including lovingly restored gypsy wagons - and to make the most of the space, he even comes up with a sofa that doubles up as a bath

programme.title: Catching a Killer
programme.desc: When elderly teacher Peter Farquhar was found dead at home in the village of Maids Moreton in 2015, it was sad and unexpected, but no one thought it suspicious. But 18 months later, when his neighbour Ann Moore-Martin also fell ill and died, the police decided to look again. They found that Ben Field, a would-be vicar and academic, had recently become part of both of their lives and benefited from their wills. This programme shows that by delving back in time through friends' and loved ones' memories of Peter and Ben's relationship, as well as Peter's diary, the police discovered a story of love and betrayal

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of the best moments from the first series, featuring celebrities including Rylan Clark-Neal and mum Linda, Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Chris Eubank and Chris Eubank Jr, Oti, Motsi and Phemelo Mabuse, Martin and Roman Kemp, and Gyles Brandreth and Sheila Hancock. Among the shows under review are Love Island, Naked Attraction, Killing Eve and First Dates, as well as the films Titanic and American Pie

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont take on Roisin Conaty and Joe Wilkinson in the comedy words-and-numbers game, while Bec Hill joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Phil Spencer returns to Brixworth in Northamptonshire to catch up with Sarah and Ian who appeared on the show back in 2015. Back then, they had just one toddler and two dogs and their two-bed semi was bursting at the seams. Ian wanted to move on, but Sarah was determined to stay, convinced their home had the potential to cater for a growing family. Now, Phil returns to find out what happened next

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: In 2018, Clare and Matt had viewed more than 30 houses around Leamington Spa, before Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer worked their magic. Now Phil's back to find things are very different from before. In 2017, the duo helped first-time buyers Louise and Johnny in expensive Weybridge. Two years on, Phil discovers that was just the start of an exciting time for this couple

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: Cornish fisherman Phil Trebilcock heads out to sea to find lobster and crab, while in the foothills of Exmoor, farmers Rob and Sarah Taylor ready their native ponies for the show season. On Lundy Island, warden Dean Jones is hopeful that the seabird colonies are making a comeback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Clough View Farm in Matlock, Derbyshire, where a long wait for breakfast provokes unease and some tough feedback is handed out by Roman and Tamsyn

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The Buxted Inn in Uckfield, East Sussex, run by Nick Bolton and chef David Kirby

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: The Spanish summer threatens work on a golf course. Setting up a glampsite in France is far from plain sailing. In Portugal, a farming business needs to find its first customers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton shows five apartments to Nottingham-based social worker Sheila, who wants to buy a holiday home on Spain's Costa Blanca

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Having just retired, Carlene Barnard wants to buy a holiday home on the Costa Blanca so that all of her family - two kids, six grandchildren and one great grandchild - can spend quality time together in the Spanish sunshine. Scarlette Douglas shows her and daughter Kerrie five properties for £90,000 in and around the seaside town of Torrevieja

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman meets Peter and Karen Williams from Shropshire, who fell in love with Portugal's eastern Algarve 12 years ago and now want to relocate there permanently. Jasmine finds them five homes suited to their £150,000 budget, including a property reduced by £50,000, and a house that feels just like their place back in the UK

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Nursing manager Sarah hosts the first of a week of dinner parties in Leeds, taking inspiration from her husband's Middle Eastern heritage to offer her guests a Turkish fusion menu. Joining her is 28-year-old painter decorator Alex, 45-year-old bridal shop owner Abbi, 60-year-old community centre manager Janet and 56-year-old record label boss Phil. A hilarious evening lies in store, with unexpected tattoos, beauty tips and twerking

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Would-be clairvoyant Janet hosts the second of a week of dinner parties in Leeds and hopes her ghoul-themed dinner party will help her win the cash prize. Part of Janet's plan is to pretend her house is haunted and she has a practical joke lined up for nursing manager Sarah and bridal shop owner Abbi to find. With more surprises in store, will the fright-night theme be enough to ghost Janet into the lead?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Record label owner Phil hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Leeds, hoping to win the £1,000 prize money by serving up a 1970s rock-themed menu. His guests are astounded by his mystery past, and painter and decorator Alex is unimpressed when wedding shop owner Abbi continues to wind him up

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Painter and decorator Alex hosts the fourth night in Leeds, hoping to win the prize money by serving up a cruise-themed menu from around the world - which impresses jealous rival Janet so much, she almost turns violent. Abbi continues to belittle the host - until he reveals he spends his evenings on stage impersonating a famous heart-throb singer. Will his performance bag him the top prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Bridal shop owner Abbi hopes to woo her guests with a wedding-themed party on the final day of the competition in Leeds. All week Abbi and Michael Buble impersonator Alex have had a bit of a banter that has sailed close to the wind. Will the tension take over, or can the wedding theme create an amorous atmosphere that will help Abbi win the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Scottsdale, Arizona, where he meets husband-and-wife bistro owners Samy and Amy Bouzaglo, who reveal a shocking contempt for customer feedback. The chef faces the biggest challenge of the series to save Amy's Baking Company, but can he get through to this defiant pair?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps a Salisbury couple who visit Malta three times a year and hope to move there permanently to find a holiday home on the island for a budget of £160,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Following a recent health scare, beauty consultant Marcelle and coach company owner Graeme are seizing the moment and about to buy a holiday home in western Malaga for stress-free holidays together. They'd love to find an apartment near the town of Benalmádena with a communal pool and private outdoor space and have given Jasmine Harman a budget of £120,000 to find the perfect property

programme.title: Mend It for Money
programme.desc: Experts restore a 19th century chair, apparently rescued from a cinema before it was demolished in the 1970s. Elsewhere, Lorna from Cumbria brings in a Victorian cot

programme.title: Jamie and Jimmy's Friday Night Feast
programme.desc: Food writer Mary Berry joins Jamie Oliver and Jimmy Doherty in their cafe on Southend Pier, where she enjoys a nostalgic seafood feast inspired by her happy family holidays in the West Country. The wander down memory lane features a stuffed whole trout, potted shrimp and a retro crab cocktail, plus bread from Mary's favourite bakery. Plus, Jimmy rustles up a gorgeously gooey mozzarella and pesto bread. Out and about, the hosts find themselves on the front line of cooking, taking part in a training exercise with the No 3 Mobile Catering Squadron at RAF Wittering

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: It's Terracotta Week in the pottery, and the potters are tasked to make their own range of cookware. Judge Rich Miller sets a second terracotta challenge to engrave tiles, before he and fellow judge Keith Brymer Jones decide who will be potter of the week and who will be leaving the competition. Siobhan McSweeney hosts

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A beauty queen bricklayer goes looking for love with a bar manager, while two students with very different priorities when it comes to conversation try to make a connection. A financial investigator meets a man who seems to have an answer to everything, and a man whose job takes him all over the world is under pressure to find himself a wife

programme.title: The Undateables
programme.desc: The series following extraordinary singletons as they search for romance returns. Nicholas, a 26-year-old from Berkshire who has Asperger's, longs to find a partner to share his life with. Meanwhile, 22-year-old Shantae, who has Down's Syndrome, goes to the Pentecostal church every Sunday with her family and dreams of meeting a religious family man. Cameras also follow Robbie Williams superfan Sam. He is 28, lives in Devon and has a global learning delay. He's a successful horse rider, competes regularly and has won scores of rosettes. Can he find Ms Right?

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: The presenter explores the UK's wildest, most remote and beautifully designed holiday homes, accompanied by some of the nation's most fascinating and funny women. This time, Sandi is joined by BAFTA-winning actor and writer Jessica Hynes to explore the ultimate staycations in Jessica's home county of Kent. The pair visit a lovingly restored early 1900s windmill outside the village of Benenden, before travelling to the Isle of Sheppey, where they explore Elmley National Nature Reserve, the only nature reserve in England where you can stay overnight

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of the best moments from the first series, featuring celebrities including Rylan Clark-Neal and mum Linda, Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Chris Eubank and Chris Eubank Jr, Oti, Motsi and Phemelo Mabuse, Martin and Roman Kemp, and Gyles Brandreth and Sheila Hancock. programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont take on Roisin Conaty and Joe Wilkinson in the comedy words-and-numbers game, while Bec Hill joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Glyn, a 60-year-old disco-dancing entertainer from Essex, hopes to find the perfect partner with whom he can dance off into the sunset. Twenty-eight-year-old Jamie has been on lots of dates, but she keeps meeting cheaters who leave her feeling cold, and needs the show's help to find an honest guy. Presented by Anna Richardson

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The Buxted Inn in Uckfield, East Sussex, run by Nick Bolton and chef David Kirby

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at the Crescent Victoria hotel in Margate, Kent, where hosts Farid Al-Faisal and John Dennis aim to treat their guests like royalty. Unfortunately, only one of the pairs feel like princesses, with the rest being less than impressed by the attempt at luxury

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In Spain, an unwanted leak wreaks havoc on an adventure golf course. Meanwhile, unsightly building work blights the start of a French gite business

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps John and Soussan search for a holiday home on Spain's Costa Calida. The pair hope to find an apartment with a pool for when their grandchildren come to visit

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Tim and Irene Foster are looking for a place to escape the cold British winters. Irene is originally from Cape Town in South Africa and has long dreamed of owning a property there so that she can see her family more often. With a budget of £240,000, Laura Hamilton shows them five properties on the Cape Peninsula

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton offers advice to Liverpool couple John and Wendy Houghton, who have been married for 43 years and have long dreamt of owning a holiday home around the Spanish coastal town of Torrevieja on the Costa Blanca. The property expert leaves no stone unturned in showing the pair how far their budget of £50,000 will stretch, with the couple viewing a two-bedroom apartment with scenic vistas and a ground-floor apartment located by a picture-perfect square

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first night in Kent, 51-year-old construction site manager and part-time bouncer Mark hopes that his dinner party can be as much about the party as it is about the dinner, as he hosts a cheesy fun-fuelled evening. Will party poppers, balloons, confetti cannons and surprising celebrity stories impress 53-year-old dog-lover Karen, 39-year-old Czech shop assistant Verush, 42-year-old singer Amrick and 58-year-old fashion photographer Anthony?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the second night in Kent, competitive sales assistant Verush is going all out to win by hosting a Czech-themed evening, but ex-stripper Mark isn't impressed, comparing Verush's pasta to MDF and pastry to leather, while the first night's argument between dog lover Karen and photographer Anthony is reignited

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dog enthusiast Karen hosts the third night of a week of dinner parties in Kent, hoping to impress her guests by serving up a menu inspired by her four-legged friends. After the cheesy fun of the previous night, she is determined to impress her guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Amrick takes his turn to host in Kent, hoping to wow his guests by revealing his musical talents and with his traditional Indian cuisine. But with dog-lover Karen and competitive Verush not liking hot and spicy food, there could be trouble - while photographer Anthony questions ex-stripper Mark's dress sense

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Photographer Anthony attempts to wow his guests with an evening of fine food and culinary excellence on the final day of the competition in Kent. However, the soiree is jeopardised by ex-stripper Mark on overdrive and dog-lover Karen showcasing some incredible impressions from animals to Anthony himself. The host might be cockily confident he can win, but Karen seemingly remains in pole position to bag the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to New Jersey's Spanish Pavilion, which is run by two warring brothers whose staff spend more time using their mobile phones than working. The chef plans to turn the eatery's fortunes around, and starts by giving the dining room and menu a modern makeover

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas helps Sunderland couple Julia and Warren find a home near the picturesque town of Álora in the Andalusian countryside, on a budget of £95,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

Danni has been given a budget of £85,000, with which she finds five properties that she thinks fits the bill. Will Amanda and Steve love one of them sufficiently to buy it?

programme.title: Mend It for Money
programme.desc: Expert restorers pitch for the chance to work on a steamer trunk, a 19th-century French daybed and an art deco drinks cabinet

programme.title: Devon and Cornwall
programme.desc: Cornish fisherman Phil Trebilcock heads out to sea to find lobster and crab, while in the foothills of Exmoor, farmers Rob and Sarah Taylor ready their native ponies for the show season. On Lundy Island, warden Dean Jones is hopeful that the seabird colonies are making a comeback

programme.title: Undercover Police: Hunting Paedophiles
programme.desc: Following a middle-aged male undercover officer as he assumes a new identity as a 13-year-old girl from Essex and begins engaging online with people who have demonstrated a sexual interest in children. Within minutes of creating an account on a social media site for teenagers, 'Keira' is bombarded with highly sexualised messages from other users, many of whom are adult men

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of the best moments from the first series, featuring celebrities including Rylan Clark-Neal and mum Linda, Nick and Liv Grimshaw, Chris Eubank and Chris Eubank Jr, Oti, Motsi and Phemelo Mabuse, Martin and Roman Kemp, and Gyles Brandreth and Sheila Hancock. Among the shows under review are Love Island, Naked Attraction, Killing Eve and First Dates, as well as the films Titanic and American Pie

programme.title: Catching a Killer
programme.desc: When elderly teacher Peter Farquhar was found dead at home in the village of Maids Moreton in 2015, it was sad and unexpected, but no one thought it suspicious. But 18 months later, when his neighbour Ann Moore-Martin also fell ill and died, the police decided to look again. They found that Ben Field, a would-be vicar and academic, had recently become part of both of their lives and benefited from their wills. This programme shows that by delving back in time through friends' and loved ones' memories of Peter and Ben's relationship, as well as Peter's diary, the police discovered a story of love and betrayal

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 13-year-old aspiring Olympian fractures his pelvis after falling from his bike, an injury that may have devastating long-term consequences for his sporting career. As doctors determine if an operation is needed, his mother reflects on how a spinal injury brought an end to her own dreams when she was a teenager. A motorcyclist is brought in to St Georges, having been flung six feet in the air following a collision with a car

programme.title: The Undateables
programme.desc: The series following extraordinary singletons as they search for romance returns. Nicholas, a 26-year-old from Berkshire who has Asperger's, longs to find a partner to share his life with. Meanwhile, 22-year-old Shantae, who has Down's Syndrome, goes to the Pentecostal church every Sunday with her family and dreams of meeting a religious family man. Cameras also follow Robbie Williams superfan Sam. He is 28, lives in Devon and has a global learning delay. He's a successful horse rider, competes regularly and has won scores of rosettes. Can he find Ms Right?

programme.title: Jamie and Jimmy's Friday Night Feast
programme.desc: Food writer Mary Berry joins Jamie Oliver and Jimmy Doherty in their cafe on Southend Pier, where she enjoys a nostalgic seafood feast inspired by her happy family holidays in the West Country. The wander down memory lane features a stuffed whole trout, potted shrimp and a retro crab cocktail, plus bread from Mary's favourite bakery. Plus, Jimmy rustles up a gorgeously gooey mozzarella and pesto bread. Out and about, the hosts find themselves on the front line of cooking, taking part in a training exercise with the No 3 Mobile Catering Squadron at RAF Wittering

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at the Crescent Victoria hotel in Margate, Kent, where hosts Farid Al-Faisal and John Dennis aim to treat their guests like royalty. Unfortunately, only one of the pairs feel like princesses, with the rest being less than impressed by the attempt at luxury

programme.title: Rich Kids Go Skint
programme.desc: Angelina, a wealthy 16-year-old, goes to live with teenage mum Amy, and learns about the responsibilities of being a mother - but ultimately cracks and checks into a hotel

programme.title: Grime and Punishment: Dirty Britain
programme.desc: The hidden army of enforcement teams and inspectors out to nail the litter louts and the fearless sewage warriors clearing the mess. Enforcement officers Dan and Jude are on the hunt for fly-tippers and rubbish dumpers blighting the beautiful countryside of the Malvern Hills, while Rob, Gary and John deal with the rivers of fat and wet wipes blocking London's sewers. In Wolverhampton, cleaners Jakob and Wojtek face a toxic clean-up thanks to someone storing 50 bottles of urine in a cupboard, while across the city a crime scene is in urgent need of a deep clean

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A landlord offers a former tenant the use of a spare room until she gets back on her feet, but then struggles to evict her - and things get even more difficult when the woman moves her partner in. A landlord travelling abroad has to return to London when her tenants stop paying the rent, and Harrow Council officers find 30 mattresses in a flat designed for four people

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Stavros and Emily try to get their house back from squatters, while Margaret attempts to recover the £20,000 she is owed from a letting agent, and Peterborough council find 12 people crammed into a category-one fire hazard house. Documentary series in which cameras follow the victims and villains of the buy-to-let boom

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers pursue international car thieves as they try to leave the country in a stolen BMW, and police chase a drink-driver through Epping Forest. In Southend, burglary suspects fleeing back to London find themselves surrounded by multiple interceptor units

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The unit's hi-tech helicopter is called on to help pursue a motorcyclist riding at speeds of up to 130mph and showing no sign of slowing down for oncoming traffic or police cars. A petrol thief risks danger when trying to destroy evidence, and an officer tests the latest weapon in the fight against crime - a Subaru Impreza STi

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The police dog unit is called into action to chase a thief seen trying to break into a lorry, and the pursuit of a Range Rover leads the interceptors to a haul of stolen prestige cars. A seized Lamborghini brings out the team's competitive streak, and the presence of an intruder puts the station on full alert

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Ben face a challenging eviction in London, where a vulnerable tenant in rent arrears has barricaded her front door, and the agents receive a tirade of abuse. Meanwhile, Brian and Del chase a debt of nearly £2,000 in Surrey, before trying to recover almost £3,000 in Northamptonshire. Finally, Steve and Ru head to Portsmouth to evict a young family for a landlord who wants her property back

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement Agents Paul and Steve are sent to north London to evict a young Romanian mother who owes £6,000 in unpaid rent, but when they arrive, they discover a family home that is not quite what it appears to be. Meanwhile, in Kent, Brian and Alan turn up the pressure on a debtor who has admitted to owing more than £7,000, but claims he cannot afford to pay up - despite having a garage full of expensive tools. Later, in south-west London, Brian accompanies Delroy as he tries to recover a £400,000 debt from a residential property, and when the pair trace the defaulter to an upmarket cafe, the man in charge lies about his identity

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: One-off edition in which the High Court enforcement agents are caught in the crossfire of three bitter family disputes, including a debt-dodging man who pushes his panic-stricken mother over the edge. Plus, a family booted onto the streets at night by a ruthless aunt, and a man who won't engage with the agents and loses his car

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Hendrix is devastated by the split with Harlow, while Jay is surprised to realise Mack might have feelings for him. Dipi and Amy end up in a lip-synch battle against each other

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Justin finally speaks to Christian, while Ziggy asks Tane to come in and help at the garage, but an armed police raid changes things. Tori works up the courage to confront Jasmine

programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell
programme.desc: Police in Hereford make anti-social pavement parking a top priority, but their operation results in emergency services finding themselves stuck in the middle. Meanwhile, At Weston-super-Mare, it's one of the busiest days of the year-the beach race day, which proves difficult for traffic enforcement officers to control

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Officers in Huntingdon explain that they had a problem with regular drunken violence at weekends, but that CCTV is at the forefront of their initiative to turn things around. Nick Wallis also discovers how CCTV is helping Chester's local council and the police manage problems on Race Day

programme.title: Countdown to Murder
programme.desc: The case of Carol Taggart, a 54-year-old mother of three, who has seemingly been murdered by her own son in Scotland

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: Psychologists and criminology experts analyse the personalities of some of the world's most infamous murderers, and consider what may have motivated them to carry out horrific crimes

programme.title: Single White Female (1992)
programme.desc: Software designer Allison is looking for a flatmate and seems to have struck lucky with shy applicant Hedra. However, Allison soon comes to realise her new friend is dangerously obsessed with her, and when she plans a reconciliation with her former boyfriend, a jealous Hedra becomes increasingly unhinged. Psychological thriller, with Bridget Fonda, Jennifer Jason Leigh, and Steven Weber

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Landlord Nicholas Parrington discovers a couple are subletting his house, just months after a previous tenant had turned the property into a brothel, while investors in a community centre for children are aghast when squatters move into their building. In Lewisham, the council's Rogue Landlord Unit raids one of their main streets, on the lookout for slum conditions, illegal overcrowding and criminal activities

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A furious landlord discovers his tenant has been subletting his exclusive north-west London flat - having not paid any rent for months. In Woolwich, squatters with a baby are given two weeks to find somewhere new to live, while a High Court enforcement officer helps an Essex grandmother evict her non-paying tenants in Dagenham

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Memorable moments from the series, including the pursuit of two knife-wielding robbers endangering both themselves and the public in a stolen Toyota. The officers also use a mixture of technology and old-fashioned legwork to chase a drug dealer whose number plate flashes up a warning

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The officers in Essex are called on to take drastic action when a pursuit from London arrives in their area. A car thief is forced to face the consequences of his actions, and a drug search in Basildon yields curious discoveries

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Members of Essex's high-speed police interception unit chase a lorry driver across three counties. Elsewhere, the team's adapted vehicles are pushed to speeds of up to 115mph while in pursuit of a joyrider. The officers also get the chance to hone their skills with the Subaru World Rally team

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In this special episode, the High Court enforcement agents have to deal with a fraudulent tenant, an evasive debtor and a mother in denial. Steve and Ben are in west London to see a tenant who owes £14,000 in unpaid rent, but when they gain access to the property they discover the house has divided up like a hotel and is being sublet. In Sussex, Brian and Del chase a debt of over £10,000 owed to a van rental business for damaged vehicles, before heading to Kent to try to reclaim £6,000 of unpaid rent from a woman financially dependent on a new partner after a disastrous divorce

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, father-and-son team Del and Dael Anglin face death threats from a debtor's father, after arriving at a property to collect a debt of over £3,500. In east London, two agents are confronted with a tenant who claims to have had no prior warning that he is being evicted from his tiny bedsit. Del and Brian try to recover £6,000 owed by a London hotel to a catering supply company, and Paul and Steve must tackle a difficult eviction

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In north London, agents Del and Dael chase nearly £2,000 owed by a printing company, but the owner denies the debt and starts getting physical. Meanwhile, Stewart and Elmor find themselves caught up in a personal dispute when they try to collect rent arrears from a barber's shop, and in Bedfordshire, Del and Brian try to recover almost £2,000 owed to a funeral director. Stewart and Vic track down a debt of £5,000 from the owner of a computer repair shop in Lincolnshire

programme.title: Neighbours

programme.title: Grime and Punishment: Dirty Britain
programme.desc: Enforcement officers Wayne and Dan continue their mission to identify the gang responsible for one of the biggest fly tips in Cardiff, more determined than ever to catch their prey. Environmental health officer Suzanne pays a visit to a takeaway restaurant in Worcester, and a London canal side is the scene of a battle between council enforcers and a waste-loving artist who has hoarded mountains of rubbish

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Documentary about the horrors of the rental market including a landlord who decided to sell his east London flat so he could afford a property with his girlfriend. But when he told his tenant, things quickly turned sour and the man stopped paying rent and refused to leave. Plus, the story of a pub owner in Lincolnshire trying to evict his tenant, having rented it 10 years ago on a 'gentleman's handshake'. Now, however, he is owed a staggering £40,000 in unpaid rent

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Brent Council officers find a severely overcrowded house in north London, while Harrow Council try to help a family forced to sleep in a single room due to leaks coming from the roof. Their landlord is reluctant to complete the work and has instead served them with an eviction notice. Plus, the case of a Birmingham landlord who wants rid of his troublesome tenants, who have not paid rent for more than a year

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The high-speed police interception unit pursues a stolen car through the suburbs of Essex until the thieves turn into a cul-de-sac and, though seemingly trapped, still try to escape. Elsewhere, officers deal with a fight outside a nightclub and tackle boy racers in Southend

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Members of Essex's high-speed police interception unit pursue a stolen BMW X5, and the Stanway team gives two unsuspecting car thieves an early morning wake-up call. Elsewhere, officers discover a vehicle that has been turned into a four-wheeled petrol bomb, and a road-tax evader encounters trouble with the law

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Four police cars pursue a vehicle speeding down the M25, an 83-year-old man fails to notice officers trying to pull him over, and the Stanway unit arrives at the scene of a car crash on a country road. The interceptors join up with the Kent force to launch a road-policing initiative that uses automatic number-plate recognition to scan vehicles on major routes

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor visit Nottingham to chase almost £8,000 owed by a car dealership to a dissatisfied customer, while on the Isle of Wight, Brian and Del try to collect £3,700 of unpaid estate agency fees. The job begins peacefully, until one of the agents is physically assaulted by the debtor's wife. In Lancashire, Stewart and Iain struggle to identify the person who owes almost £2,000 after a failed compensation claim, while Del and Dael try to recover nearly £3,500 from a businessman in west London

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, Del and Dael Anglin receive a hostile reception from the brother of a debtor who has alreasy defaulted on a promise to pay £2,000 she owes to a storage company. In Worcestershire, Gareth and Craig try to recover £45,000 from a young online entrepreneur, who claims no responsibility for the debt he owes to his courier. Their target cooks up a flimsy lie in an effort to absolve himself, but dishonesty proves to be the least of the agents' worries when they discover a stash of guns at the back of the garage. In east London, an Indian restaurant owner begins to take his financial responsibilities seriously when Del and Dael start removing cookers and freezers from his premises, and a pair of pensioners in Oxfordshire hope the High Court will provide them a lifeline as they try to pay off a £4,000 bill to a solicitors' firm after a failed compensation claim

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gareth and Craig investigate a possible case of mistaken identity in Shropshire, then in south Wales find themselves embroiled in a bitter dispute between two dog owners over a vet's bill. Steve and Phil have to evict a distraught young family from a former high street bank in west London, before paying a call on an elderly tailor in north London

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Bea is unaware of how much peril she is in after eating a pie with toxic mushrooms. At their baby's first ultrasound, Aaron and David decide to trust Nicolette again, to her relief

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Ziggy tells Dean there is no chance they will get back together, while Jasmine bumps into an old friend from university at the prison. Martha tells Alf about Kieran's father

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Chris and Gary have decided to change everything for a new life in Spain, arriving with just two suitcases, a hotel for a few nights and £3,500. But having never been to Benidorm before, could their dream turn into a nightmare? Scotsman Graham has hit upon the idea of selling organised pub crawls, while another Graham - the drivetime DJ at Fresh Radio - faces a dilemma when he is offered his old job back, which would mean spending less time with girlfriend Jackie

programme.title: Casualty 24/7
programme.desc: A two-year-old is rushed to hospital, struggling to breathe, and consultant and part-time stand-up comic Dr Rob Jones is forced to put his comedy skills to good use keeping the distressed child relaxed. Other patients include a miner who has suffered a suspected heart attack and a 98-year-old in need of a CT scan

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Paramedic Jack attends the scene of a double stabbing in Birmingham, where two neighbours have been attacked. Plus, Ambulance Control receive an emergency call reporting a man trapped under a bus

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: The return of the documentary going behind the scenes at Hull Royal Infirmary to show the night-time challenges facing NHS staff working in the A&E department. A patient in police custody is brought in for treatment to a split eyebrow and some facial wounds, while the resus team attempts to save the life of a patient who is rushed into hospital in cardiac arrest

programme.title: The Disappearance
programme.desc: Susan and Charles make a mysterious discovery in the park, and the investigation into Anthony's disappearance is reopened after two years

programme.title: Bargain Brits on Benefits
programme.desc: Cameras follow folks whose clever ideas and savvy schemes save them a small fortune

programme.title: Filthy Britain SOS
programme.desc: Anna and John have to work hard to get to the root of an almighty blockage threatening to send a tsunami of waste gushing into a bungalow. In Lewes, East Sussex, after one of the biggest bonfire night celebrations in the country, filth fighters reveal the trade secrets of clearing up the mess left by the 80,000 revellers

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The murder of a Middle Eastern man sends Briscoe and Curtis racing to thwart a suspected terrorist plot to bring destruction to New York - and leads them to uncover a domestic conspiracy involving horrific female circumcision. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy faces bad memories when a drunk driver kills three people - and finds himself forced to protect the perpetrator in the process. Award-winning police drama, starring Benjamin Bratt and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Cutter is forced to face the unexpected consequences of his actions when his guilty verdict in a murder trial is set aside. Anna Chlumsky guest stars, with Linus Roache

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the murders of four policemen and discover a connection between drug dealers, a jealous husband and a remorseful judge

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Curtis and Briscoe question a disabled boy's bereaved family in an attempt to establish whether his death was a mercy killing or cold-blooded murder, and discover his mother is not as respectable as she makes herself out to be. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A female cop is found stabbed to death in a park alongside a Hispanic male with a near-fatal gunshot wound. Further investigations lead Briscoe and Curtis to a girl who suffered a tough childhood in the care of an abusive father and drug-addicted mother, and her alibi appears to be flimsy at best. Robert Vaughn guest stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Logan are approached by a woman who claims she passed out in a taxi and awoke to find her newborn baby missing. Investigating her allegation, they uncover a gang who specialise in kidnapping children to order. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Christopher Noth

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Logan investigate a respected judge's ex-lover and her child who are being terrorised by someone rather too close to home. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Christopher Noth

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A nuclear power plant inspector suffers radiation poisoning, forcing the team to consider the possibility that a foreign power deliberately targeted him. As the others investigate, Ziva spends time with the ailing victim - and begins to realise she is developing feelings for him. Drama, guest starring Matthew Marsden, with Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An explosion at a mausoleum exposes a number of freshly dismembered bodies, prompting the team to launch a multiple murder investigation to identify the victims. Meanwhile, Abby has a personal problem to deal with - and it seems Gibbs is the only person she will confide in. Drama, starring Pauley Perrette and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A supposedly dead marine suddenly regains consciousness on the autopsy table, leaving the team struggling to solve the mystery of how he came to be in such a strange predicament. Investigations reveal he had recently been on a trip to Baghdad - and uncover an unexpected link to Gibbs' past. Drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Goren and Bishop try to track down a latter-day Bonnie and Clyde who have already killed nine people, including two undercover cops. There is little evidence to go on, except that the officers were looking into an illegal gun-running ring. Crime drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Samantha Buck

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Newspaper columnist Jimmy MacArthur is given full access to the squad for an inside look at the New York Police Department. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hargitay, Danny Pino and Ice-T

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A single mother faces charges of child neglect after her six-year-old daughter is discovered home alone. However, Benson suspects there is more to her story and his investigations reveal she not only suffered sexual abuse as a child but also featured in scores of images and videos popular with online paedophiles - forcing the woman to face up to her past. Starring Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Rollins helps her friend press charges against her lover, whom she accuses of rape. However, when the case escalates to a charge of murder, Rollins finds herself forced to reveal details of her own private life on the stand. Hard-hitting drama, starring Kelli Giddish, Mariska Hargitay and Dann Florek

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Unfortunately, the object of their suspicion has a clever defence already up her sleeve - and only an expert witness can break her story. Sam Waterston and Carey Lowell star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A charity worker with a secret appetite for sordid encounters dies at the hands of a serial killer, but the defendant constructs a unique case by claiming the crime was the result of exposure to excessive violence on television. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Carey Lowell

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the death of an environmental scientist who was poisoned at a global-warming convention. The case bringing up a number of suspects, including professional rivals and his wife's former husband, and for Cutter it stirs up unpleasant memories from his own past

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Bernard investigate the death of an heiress from an apparent drug overdose. However, the duo soon realise there is more to the case than meets the eye, as they uncover a conspiracy to kill off wealthy cancer patients so inheritors do not have to pay an estate tax

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A newly appointed judge is knocked over and killed while trying to escape from the tabloid journalist who has been harassing her. The team is all set to press charges against the reporter, but it soon becomes clear there is more to the case than meets the eye. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Cyrus Lupo returns to New York after four years abroad. He and Green investigate two suicides that seem to be linked, and the detectives become suspicious of a parolee known as Dr Death. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jesse L Martin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A 14-year-old boy is killed in a drive-by shooting, and investigatations lead Logan to a local gun dealer who is the suspect in another recent incident. However, things become complicated for Briscoe when the son of an old friend is also implicated in the crime

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman is killed by a letter-bomb, and her scientist husband becomes the chief suspect when it is discovered that the couple were planning to divorce, but the victim was intentionally delaying the proceedings

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The unit is tipped off about terrorist activity, and two agents are murdered during the ensuing investigation. Believing the information to be a set-up, the team tries to solve the case with assistance from a guilt-stricken Paula Cassidy, who holds herself responsible for the tragedy. Drama, starring Jessica Steen

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee feels the pressure as the team investigates a double murder that appears to be inspired by his still unpublished new novel. His sense of guilt is compounded by the realisation that one of his colleagues could become the killer's third target. American drama following the work of the Naval Criminal Investigative Service, starring Sean Murray and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Jenny Shepard tries to solve the murder of an underworld source who was prepared to give information on the activities of arms dealer La Grenouille. Unfortunately her behaviour becomes increasingly obsessive as the case proceeds, leaving Gibbs wondering if her judgement is being clouded by a personal motivation. Starring Lauren Holly and Mark Harmon

Starring Mariska Hargitay and Pablo Schreiber

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Rollins and Benson investigate the rape of an underage girl, who initially refuses to co-operate or press charges against a trio of suspects, but eventually comes clean and leads the detectives to the strip club where she works - although her boss denies any wrongdoing and rejects a request for his client list. Jeremy Sisto and Anthony Anderson star in penultimate episode of the long-running crime drama

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Green embark on their first official case together, investigating the kidnapping of a mother and daughter which occurred during a city-wide blackout. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is made acting director while Jenny is attending an overseas conference. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.desc: An injured Ash is rushed to hospital, his confidence shattered - but an emergency forces him to return to duty. Final episode of the Second World war drama

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest (2002)
programme.desc: Two bachelors using the same pseudonym capture the hearts of a couple of girls during a delightful weekend in the countryside. However, their cunning deception leads to all manner of hilarious confusion as the ladies in question attempt to work out who is who. Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's comedy, starring Rupert Everett, Colin Firth, Frances O'Connor, Reese Witherspoon, Judi Dench and Tom Wilkinson

programme.desc: A tyrannically strict Lancashire father brings about the downfall of his family by forcing his daughter to eat a meal against her will. Drama, starring James Mason, Diana Coupland, Susan George and Rodney Bewes

programme.title: 50 Shades of Gay
programme.desc: Rupert Everett explores the changes in gay life in the 50 years since the decriminalisation of male homosexuality in England and Wales in 1967. He meets LGBT people from all walks of life, including former royal butler Paul Burrell, rapper Jai'Rouge, actors from Hollyoaks, and Essex carpenter Steve, and visits Hebden Bridge in West Yorkshire, known as the lesbian capital of Britain

programme.title: Golden Years (2016)
programme.desc: A retired couple face a grim future after a financial crisis costs them both their pensions. Refusing to take the situation lying down, they resolve to take back what is rightfully theirs with a series of daring bank robberies. Comedy, starring Bernard Hill, Virginia McKenna, Simon Callow and Sue Johnston

programme.title: Death Trench (2017)
programme.desc: At the end of the First World War, a squad of soldiers investigates an abandoned German underground base. They soon face a fight for survival against the victims of an experimental biological weapon, who have been transformed into frenzied bloodthirsty killers. Horror, starring Rossif Sutherland and Robert Stadlober

programme.title: Helga, She Wolf of Stilberg (1978)
programme.desc: A sadistic female Nazi officer runs a women's prison during the Second World War, and forces the inmates to be her sexual playthings. However, the latest arrival, a determined resistance fighter, proves to be far more difficult to dominate. Prison drama, starring Malisa Longo and Patrizia Gori. In French

programme.desc: Highlights from Brent 2020's No Bass Like Home Festival celebrating reggae culture. Featuring live music sets and the musical, cultural and social history of reggae in and around Harlesden

programme.title: Inside Claridge's
programme.desc: Thirty delegations from around the world arrive to stay at the hotel for the duration of the Olympics, with the red carpet being rolled out five times a day for heads of state. World-famous Danish eatery Noma sets up a pop-up restaurant in the ballroom and Claridge's chefs are faced with providing Nordic-foraged food to thousands of guests who have paid £195 each. A couple fly in from Atlanta for the wedding of their dreams and a butler prepares a suite for one of the hotel's most valued guests, bringing furniture, hat boxes and 20 suits out of storage for just one night

programme.title: Madame Tussaud's Revealed
programme.desc: 200 years ago, Madame Tussaud created the most successful waxwork museum ever. This documentary reveals some of the magnificent works on display and the amazing methods used to create the exhibits

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: Street gang members Passion and Little Sister are released from prison, only to find that their psychotic group has disbanded in their absence. Nevertheless, they manage to get the Slags regrouped and begin planning a showdown with benevolent rivals the Hawaiians. However, matters become complicated when Little Sister falls in love with their leader. Comedy drama, with Jennifer Saunders and Dawn French

programme.desc: A young woman dreams of following in her mother's footsteps and becoming an actress, but along the way becomes involved in a love triangle with a charming director and a penniless playwright. Romantic drama set in the 1940s, starring Zoe Tapper, Anjelica Huston, Lauren Bacall and Terence Stamp

programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War thriller following the exploits of a squadron of British officers who head into Nazi-occupied Crete to kidnap a German general from under his comrades' noses, but struggle to transport their prisoner back to Cairo unnoticed. Starring Dirk Bogarde, Marius Goring and Cyril Cusack. Directed by frequent collaborators Michael Powell and Emeric Pressburger

programme.title: Brief Encounter (1974)
programme.desc: Two married strangers meet by chance in a railway station waiting room, and their paths later cross many times until the pair become friends and their meetings a fixed arrangement. But as their feelings for each other transform from friendship into passion, both come to realise the affair is doomed to fail. Remake of David Lean's 1948 classic, starring Richard Burton and Sophia Loren

programme.desc: A man who fled his home town to escape his abusive father decides to return once he receives word that the old man is dead. However, he faces a bitter reunion with the brother, who stayed behind to care for their father in his last days, and his ex-girlfriend, whom he abandoned with no explanation. Drama, starring Marty Denniss, Hugh Jackman and Joel Edgerton

programme.desc: A man ends up in over his head in the world of organised crime when his girlfriend is kidnapped by an underground prostitution ring. His attempts to raise the ransom money land him a job delivering dead bodies for a crime syndicate. Crime thriller, starring Brett Newton and Victor Kelso

programme.title: Emmanuelle in Soho (1981)
programme.desc: A woman seeking fame and fortune in Soho plays a pivotal role in the struggling marriage of a photographer and his wife, and conspires with them on a plot to blackmail a pornography producer. Erotic drama, starring Angie Quick and Julie Lee

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: In a Coronation Street special, Charlie Condou (Marcus Dent) and his family compete against Paula Lane (Kylie Platt) and her relatives, answering a series of questions based on a survey of 100 members of the public. A charity prize pot of up to £30,000 is up for grabs for the team that reaches the final round. Vernon Kay hosts the game show

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: A team from Dancing on Ice - Torvill and Dean, Christine Bleakley, Ashley Roberts and Robin Cousins - and some of the stars of Coronation Street - Antony Cotton (Sean), Alison King (Carla), Cherylee Houston (Izzy), Jennie McAlpine (Fiz) and Catherine Tyldesley (Eva) - compete for the chance to win money for charity. A jackpot of £30,000 is up for grabs as the contestants try to guess the answers to surveys of 100 members of the British public. Vernon Kay hosts this festive special of the long-running game show

programme.title: EFL Highlights
programme.desc: Crystal Palace v Watford. Action from the 2012/13 Championship play-off final at Wembley Stadium, as the teams contested a place in the top flight alongside the already promoted Cardiff City and Hull City. Palace reached this match when Wilfried Zaha scored a brace in a 2-0 away win against Brighton & Hove Albion after a goalless draw in the first leg. Meanwhile, Watford bounced back from narrowly missing out on automatic promotion by overcoming Leicester City in dramatic fashion, with Troy Deeney scoring an injury time-winner on the counter after Manuel Almunia had saved a penalty

programme.title: NFL: America's Game
programme.desc: The story of San Francisco 49ers' 1988 Super Bowl success over Cincinnati Bengals at the Joe Robbie Stadium in Miami, Florida. This game is remembered for the 49ers' winning drive in the last few minutes of the game when, trailing 16-13 and with the ball on their own eight-yard line, quarterback Joe Montana led his team down the field and threw the winning touchdown pass with seconds to spare

programme.desc: Revisiting Dallas Cowboys' 1995 Super Bowl victory over Pittsburgh Steelers at the Sun Devil Stadium in Tempe, Arizona.
